Clothe them with the attributes of Thy loving-kindness and adorn them with the manners of the spiritual assembly, so that they may become manifestations of the signs of Thy oneness and evidences of the effulgent light of the unique Lord, and causes of the glory of Thy Cause and the exaltation of their own station, that their extremity may be changed into ease, and their poverty into riches; that they may gain eternal glory and everlasting favour, become manifestations of holiness and the dawning-places of sanctity, and recount the accounts of the world of lights and spirituality, so that all doors may be opened unto them from all directions. Verily, Thou art the Powerful, the Mighty.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

fa.cat: محافل روحانی مجامع و جلسات عمومی

prayers.text:
Thou art the Almighty, the Forgiver, the Merciful! O my Lord, my Lord! Thou seest Thy servants in the depths of hell and error. O Desire of the world, where is the Light of Thy guidance? Thou knowest that they are weak and unable to help themselves. O Thou whose dominion over heaven and earth is in Thy hand, where is Thy dominion?

O my Master, my Lord! I beseech the light of the lamp of Thy grace, the waves of the ocean of Thy knowledge, the utterance with which Thou rulest the people in Thy kingdom. Grant that I may be among those who follow the command in Thy Book, decree for me that which Thou hast decreed for them whom Thou trustest, who have drunk the immortal water of inspiration from the cup of Thy favour. Hasten to do what pleaseth Thee, cling to Thy will and Thy decree. Thou art powerful to do what Thou wishest. There is no God but Thee, the All-knowing, the All-wise.

O my Master, let Thy favour manifest unto me that which will make me radiant in this world and the world to come, and bring me unto Thee. O Lord of mankind, there is no God but Thee, the One, the Almighty, the Praised One.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is God.

O Lord, forgiving Lord! These are the broken-winged birds, grant them the wings of Thy favour and let them soar in the heavens of Thy mercy. These are the helpless creatures, give them the sustenance of Thy love and let them rest in the shadow of Thy forgiveness. These are the thirsty ones, bring them to the fount of Thy grace and let them drink. These are the needy ones, present them at the table of Thy bounty.

Deliver them from the dark night of separation and gladden their eyes with the dawn of reunion. Pardon their sins, bestow upon them Thy gifts, and fulfil what Thou hast promised.

Though we are sinners, we hope in Thy forgiveness; though we are without talent, we have the utmost trust in Thy justice.

Thou art the Bounteous, the Giver, the Forgiving, the Merciful.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is God.
O Incomparable Lord! We are needy and worship You. Make these servants aware of Your secret and grant us the ability to hear the unseen voice from the highest heaven.

Keep us firm on the path of Your covenant and Your promise, and make us companions in the remembrance of Your blessed Beauty. Give us to drink from the cup of Your Oneness, and let us taste the honey of Your favor, and bestow upon us Your ancient Beauty's great bounty. Guide this servant of Your threshold towards the court of Your Oneness, and do not disappoint the one who hopes for Your mercy, and keep us firmly established on the covenant and faith.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is the Glory.
O Lord, Thy friends in every corner are under the threat of strangers and adversaries, weary of life and set aside from every thought, seeking Thy face and moving towards Thee, speaking Thy secret.

O kind Beloved, protect and guard the helpless from strangers in the fortress of Thy protection, and uplift the fallen by the hand of Thy power. Grant abundant portion to the deprived from Thy ancient bounty, make the dispossessed confidants of Thy secrets, and nurture and bestow upon the longing ones the gift of Thy vision, for they are captive to those musk-scented tresses, and at every breath they offer a hundred lives.

Thou art the Mighty and the Capable, and Thou art the Giver, the Bountiful, and the All-Seeing.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
**He is the All-Bountiful**

O kind Lord, lay hold of these prisoners of Thy love, and be their refuge, shelter, and protector. Send Thou the holy breaths from the rose-garden of Thy favour, make the courts of the hearts a rose-garden of bestowal and a meadow of reality, make them weary of all save Thyself, and cause them to commune with Thee in intimate worship, make the insignificant ant a Solomon of the magnificent dominion, and the humble atom a prince of the highest heaven, grant the drop the favour of the ocean, and give the blade of grass the freshness and beauty of the verdant tree, all are Thy friends and servants at Thy threshold; bestow Thy grace and bounty, and in this blessed day make Thine especial confirmation manifest. Thou art the Bountiful, the Merciful, the Radiant, and the Shining.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

fa.cat: محافل روحانی مجامع و جلسات عمومی

prayers.text:
He is God

O loving Lord, though we are needy children and powerless babes, distressed and without a helper, yet we are the tender saplings of Thy garden, and Thou art our Gardener. O my Lord, by the bounty of the cloud of Thy favour, the heat of the Sun of Truth, and the gentle breezes of Thy guidance, nurture these saplings, bestow upon them freshness and tenderness, so that they may bear leaves and blossoms, and yield abundant fruit. O compassionate Lord, make us knowing, though we are ignorant; make us seeing, though we are without eyes; make us living, though we are dead; assist us, for we are without helpers, and be gracious unto us. Make us successful in learning and teaching, and confirm us in acquiring and perfecting. Thou art the Powerful, the Almighty, the Bestower, the Forgiving, and the Merciful.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Thy test is the medicine of Thy nearness, Thy decree the desire of Thy lovers, Thy arrows the yearning of Thy seekers, and Thy will the hope of Thy believers.

I beg of Thee, by Thy sacred essence and the light of Thy countenance, from Thy habitation on high, to bestow upon us all the grace of drawing nigh unto Thee. And thereafter, O my God, make us to be steadfast in Thy covenant, illumine our hearts with the radiance of Thy knowledge, and gladden our breasts with the light of Thy name.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text: O thou who art guided by the light of guidance! The Sun of Truth hath shed the rays of guidance upon thy tender heart, and the glass of thy heart hath been illumined [3.5s]

He is the Glory of God
O Lord,
Grant favour, bestow guidance, nurture, grant growth and development,
Make me a daughter of the Kingdom, bestow the grace of the Divine realm, make me spiritual, make me luminous, make me merciful, make me heavenly,
so that I may be freed from the world of nature and gain knowledge of the world beyond nature,
may I be enlivened, may I seek the character and disposition of the Merciful,
may I spread the divine teachings, may I seek eternal grace and desire the divine gift.
Thou art the Giver, the Bestower, and the Kind.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is God

Pure Creator, we are all sinners and You are the Forgiver, we are all full of faults and You are the Remover of distress.
Do not record the sins of these sinners in the preserved tablet, and erase them from the inscribed scroll.
Do not look at (our) worthiness and capacity, but deal with us according to what is worthy of the threshold of the All-Merciful.
Forgiveness is a glorious attribute, and pardon and clemency are among the greatest perfections of that Majestic Lord.
So forgive the sins of these wretched ones and overlook the shortcomings of these sinners.
The sin of this servant is the greatest of sins, and the sin of this helpless one is greater than a majestic mountain.
First, forgive the shortcomings of this one without provision, then the sins of every ignorant sinner.
You are the Bountiful and the Merciful, and You are the Forgiver and the Helper.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

fa.cat: محافل روحانی مجامع و جلسات عمومی

prayers.text:
O Lord, my Lord!
This is the day which Thou hast consecrated by Thy Holy tongue and pen of glory, and made it a day of heavenly blessings, and of divine clemency and favour. Thou hast honored it with Thy gifts, bestowed upon it Thy grace, enwrapped it in Thy effulgence, and made it a day of joy and gladness unto such as have turned towards Thy divine revelation, that their hearts may rejoice, and the radiance of felicity may illumine the lives of them that remember the manifestation of Thy glory on Thy holy Mount.
Consecrate it, O Lord, by Thy favour, and make it a day of joy and of blessing unto Thy loved ones, who have remained steadfast and unmoved in Thy covenant, Thy friends who have sung Thy praises, and specially unto this Thy servant, who hath turned his face towards Thy holy kingdom, sought the glory of Thy might and Thy sovereignty, circled round the throne of Thy majesty, and bowed himself down on the earth at the threshold of Thy divine mercy.
Thou art the Gracious, the Bountiful, the Most Merciful.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text: O my God, by Thy power, Thy might, and Thy sovereignty which gather all those who are on earth and in heaven, I implore Thee to make known to Thy servants this luminous way and this straight path. May they recognize Thy unity and Thy uniqueness with such certainty that it is not shaken by the vain ideas of skeptics nor obscured by the futile imaginations of the obstinate! O my Lord, illuminate the eyes of Thy servants and enlighten their hearts by the splendors of the light of Thy knowledge. May they thus understand the greatness of this sublime rank and recognize this luminous horizon so that the clamor of men may not divert their gaze from the resplendent light of Thy unity, nor their faces from the horizon of detachment!
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text: O my God, O my God! I beseech Thee by Thy hidden confirmations, Thy divine bounties, and Thy merciful outpourings [3.5s]

O Kind God,
Your helpers are without shelter and Your friends are captive in the hands of oppressors.
From every direction, arrows and spears, and from every person, relentless wounds.
Despite this, they are lost in Your path, distraught by Your essence, and devoted to Your countenance.
They consider every calamity as divine favour, and every pain as a cure.
They find shackles and chains as a crown and throne, and consider prison an endless courtyard; they offer their lives entirely.

O Lord, favour the wise and make them companions and confidants of ʻAbdu'l-Bahá, so that we, holding soul and heart in our palms, may offer them as a sacrifice at Your threshold, seek Your countenance, find Your path, and speak Your secret.
You are the Almighty, the Capable, the Aware, and the All-seeing.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is God

O Everlasting, Almighty One, refuge of sinners, Bountiful and Merciful One, we are drowned in error, and Thou art the Ocean of Generosity; we are in the darkness of disobedience, and Thou art the Sun of Grace and Kindness.

O Lord, turn not away from our shortcomings, and deal not with us according to our exceeding weakness and inability; we are abased and weak, make us honored, grant us glory, bestow healing, shower upon us the greatest bounty of pardon and forgiveness.

O Forgiver, take us not to task for our transgressions; Thou art the Helper and the Guide of every bewildered one. Thou hast made us weary of the material world; make us triumphant in the spiritual kingdom; Thou hast delivered us from the realm of calamities, bring us to the rose-garden of Thy favours.

Thou art the Bountiful, the Giver, the Deliverer, the Everlasting, the Kind.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is the All-Glorious

O Lord, O Merciful, these friends abide at Thy threshold, and these companions are flames burning from the heat of love. In remembrance of Thee, they are like the surging sea, and in the assembly of Divine Unity, like a brilliant lamp. Each is a wave in the ocean of Thy grace, and each a casket of the pearls of Thy guidance. At dawn, with a knowing heart, they weep and lament, and at night, in remembrance of Thy beauty, they sleep and find rest. Thou art the Generous and the Merciful, and Thou art the Everlasting and the Most Compassionate. O Thou Self-Sufficient, forgive the dead, and favour those who remain. Be the cure for the helpless, and grant refuge and safety to the free. Overlook our shortcomings, and forgive our sins. Thou art the Veiler (of faults), Thou art the Forgiver.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

fa.cat: محافل روحانی مجامع و جلسات عمومی

prayers.text:
Alláh'u'Abhá

O Unique God, make this servant of Yours the adornment of the essence of being, and crown this servant with the crown of servitude among the concourse of creation. Satisfy this destitute one with Your bounty alone, and grant this one without means stability in Your protection. Let him drink from the cup of detachment, and make him ecstatic from the chalice of Your grace. What can a weak servant do, and what can a frail gnat fly? Where is the height of glory, and where are the wings of the bird of abasement? O Lord, You confirm, You grant success, verily You are the Generous.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Huwalláh
O Lord, these souls yearn for the Vision, and the frenzied lovers of that musk-scented tress, each moment cry out, and raise a lament and wail, and ask for a favour, and seek a gift; Thou art the Bountiful, the Merciful, and Thou art the Compassionate, the All-Merciful, and Thou art the Bestower of endless grace. O Lord, the Bountiful, make these souls steadfast in their covenant, firm in their covenant, draw them to the Divine Fragrances of holiness, remind them of the signs of Thy unity, make them recipients of the moments of Thy perfect mercy, and enamoured of the beauty of Thy effulgent light, grant them fresh confirmations each moment, and waft to them, with every breath, the breezes of Thy grace. Thou art potent and powerful, and Thou art the Bestower, the Generous, the All-Knowing.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O God, the Merciful, the Gracious!
Attain unto the Source of eternal life and be filled with the pure water from the 'purest stream'. Admit Thy servants into the highest paradise and make them to be intoxicated with the wine of the 'cup of a mixture of camphor'. Illumine the hearts with the lights of Thy mercy, gladden the eyes, grant unto souls the inspiration of the living, Almighty God, and bestow upon spirits the descent of Divine knowledge. Open the celestial doors unto the hearts of Thy loved ones and make them to be favoured with the Greater Guidance. Grant the poor a portion from the ocean of Thy bounty, and by the drops from Thy boundless sea, bestow freshness and tenderness upon the beings of the created world. Thou art the Bountiful, the Almighty, the All-Knowing, the All-Seeing.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O God, my God. Turn me not away from the heaven of Thy grace and the Daystar of Thy favour. I implore Thee by Thy Word which hath subdued all that are in the heavens and all that are on the earth. Aid me, and them that have chosen Thy path, to achieve that which Thou didst ordain for the exaltation of Thy Cause amongst Thy servants, and in all lands under Thy dominion. Appoint unto me all good that Thou hast revealed in Thy Holy Book.

Verily, Thou art the All-Powerful, the Ever-Forgiving, the Most Generous.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
God is the Glory
O Lord, I am a sinner and Thou art the Forgiver. Intoxicated with the wine of heedlessness, grant me sobriety. Occupied with the remembrance of this sorrowful world, grant me an ear to hear the melodies of the Highest Assembly, to listen to the cry of Thy holy nightingale, and to perceive with mighty sense the fragrances of thankful birds.

O Lord, remove the veil from my eyes that I may behold Thy Great Signs and perceive the subtleties of Thy Majesty's mysteries. Bestow a spirit upon my heart and grant my soul a spirit and a heavenly breeze that may befitting this great century and worthy of this glorious age.

Glory be upon Thee.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is God
O Lord, Thou didst bring these wanderers into Thy shadow and give them a home. Thou didst give being to non-being, and existence to the annihilated. Thou didst give pure water to the thirsty, and spread a bounteous table for the hungry, and freely didst provide all bounty. Be Thou, therefore, in all circumstances a refuge and sanctuary, and in all aims a Helper and a Succorer. Thou art the Almighty and the All-Powerful. Thou art the Giver, the Hearer, the Powerful, and the Seer.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

fa.cat: راز و نياز- شكر و ستايش

prayers.text:
Baha'u'llah
O God! Accept this humble maidservant in Thy Kingdom and gladden her with eternal bounty. Grant her the cup of Thy love, and bestow upon her the light of Thy knowledge. Immerse her in the fount of Job, and heal her from the trials and pains of human character. Bestow upon her a ray of Thy attributes; make her heavenly, make her divine. Breathe into her the Holy Spirit; quicken her with the spirit of the unity of the human race. Grant her an eloquent tongue and a detached heart. Inspire her with proofs and arguments, and cause her to be a guide unto the peoples. Thou art powerful and capable; Thou art Bountiful, Merciful, and All-knowing.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text: O thou who art turning unto God! Close thine eyes to all else and open them to the kingdom of the All-Glorious. Unto Him, exclusively, pray thou for whatsoever thou dost desire; from Him, exclusively, seek thou whatsoever thou seekest. With a glance, He realizeth a hundred thousand hopes; in a twinkling, He cureth a hundred thousand incurable ills; with a glimpse, He applieth a balm to every wound; with a nod, He liberateth the hearts from the fetters of suffering. Thus He proceedeth, and what recourse have we? He fulfilleth His will; He commandeth whatsoever He pleaseth. So, it is better for thee to bow thy head in submission, and to place thy trust in the Lord of all-mercifulness.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is the Most Glorious, O God, Most Glorious!
13 O God, this is a bird that hath flown to the meadows of Thy forgiveness and pardon, and this a breeze that hath returned to the garden of Thy grace and bounty, and this a servant who hath sought the proximity of Thy mercy. O my Lord, honour his station and make him to abide in the shadow of the Tree of Bliss, and open to him the gates of union, and grant him the highest degree, and encompass him with the splendours of Thy great favours, and clothe him with the robes of bestowal, for Thou art indeed the Bountiful, the Merciful.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is the All-Glorious!
O Lord! Grant shelter unto this helpless servant in Thy mighty stronghold and beneath Thy lofty protection. Kindle a bright candle in his heart and cause a spring of Salsabil (a fountain of Paradise) to flow from his soul. Open the doors of Thy grace before his face and inspire his heart with the mysteries of the Unseen. Thou art the Helper, the Sustainer, the Protector, the Succorer. When we turn unto Thee, we are freed from all things; when we are firm and steadfast in Thy Covenant and Faith, we are detached from all else but Thee. O Lord! Have mercy and grant us steadfastness.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is the All-Glorious,
O Lord, make this gathering a shining lamp and confirm these souls, make the faces radiant and the dispositions a garden and paradise, quicken the spirits with the breaths of holiness and adorn the beings with the great guidance, bestow heavenly forgiveness and grant abundant merciful gifts, and preserve and protect them in the sanctuary of Thy care and protection, include endless favours and perfect special bestowals.
Thou art the Giver, the Bestower, and the All-Knowing.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

fa.cat: محافل روحانی مجامع و جلسات عمومی

prayers.text:
He is the All-Glorious.

O my loving God! Through pure bounty Thou didst breathe the spirit of existence and bestow the garment of life. Thou didst cause [me] to drink from the fountain of guidance and didst cause the call of "Am I not [your Lord]?" to reach the ear of this servant. Thou didst cause the cry of "Glory be to my Lord, the All-Glorious" to be heard and didst grant shelter beneath the shade of the Tree of Anisa. Thou didst cause [me] to drink from the cup of the Covenant, didst distinguish [me] with special favor, and didst enable [me] to attain the supreme triumph.

Now, O Lord, make this poor one a manifest banner and illumine this sorrowful one in the radiant horizon. Thou art the Bounteous, the Compassionate.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)
(LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is the Glory

O Pure Creator, bestow confirmation and grant success, that we may forget stranger and kin, and be stilled from every remembrance, and be moved with ardour by the breezes of the gardens of Thy holiness. O Lord, make Thy servant a brilliant sign and a celebrated banner, a shining star and a luminous planet; heal every pain and be the balm of grace and bounty for every wound; make a bright candle for every gathering and a sweet-speaking parrot in every assembly.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O my God! O my Lord! These are Thy servants who have turned their faces towards Thy Kingdom, and have set their hearts upon the horizon of Thy mercy. They are the shining lamps of Thy guidance, the brilliant stars of Thy grace, and the mirrors of Thy beauty. They are the signs of Thy oneness, the standards of Thy manifest religion, and the servants of Thy Great Covenant.

O our exalted Lord! Make them as seas rolling with the waves of Thy great abundance, rivers flowing from the mountains of Thy glorious Kingdom, pure fruits on the tree of Thy illustrious Cause, plants refreshed and moved by the breeze of Thy gift in Thy wonderful vineyard.

O Lord! Make them as shining lamps, glittering stars, brilliant signs, and as souls attracted to the Holy Spirit.

Verily, Thou art the Munificent, the Merciful, the Precious, the Most Beloved.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text: I bitte Dich, o mein Gott, bei Deiner Kraft und Deiner Herrschaft, die alle in Deinem Himmel und auf Deiner Erde umfasst, zeige Deinen Dienern diesen leuchtenden Weg und diesen geraden Pfad, damit sie Deine Einheit und Einzigkeit mit einer Gewissheit erkennen, die weder der eitle Wahn der Zweifler schmälern noch der leere Trug der Widerspenstigen verdunkeln kann. Erleuchte, o mein Herr, die Augen Deiner Diener und erheitere ihr Herz mit dem Lichtglanz Deiner Erkenntnis, damit sie die Größe dieser erhabensten Stufe erfassen und diesen strahlenden Horizont erkennen, so dass die Menschen mit ihrem Geschrei sie nicht mehr davon abhalten, ihren Blick auf das strahlende Licht Deiner Einheit zu richten, noch sie hindern, ihr Angesicht dem Horizonte der Loslösung zuzuwenden.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is God.

O Lord, this sinner is ashamed and entangled by the vain imaginations of this dark and gloomy dust-heap.

O kind Beloved, rid me of self and desire, and draw me into Thy love, so that I may pass beyond myself, seek Thy face, desire Thy disposition, walk Thy path, become free from every tie, and become joyful from every sorrow and calamity. Thou art the forgiving, the giver, and the merciful.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is the All-Glorious.

O God, grant wings and feathers to this bird of the garden of divine knowledge, and bestow nearness and station upon this butterfly of Your lamp of love. Guide this wanderer of the desert of love to the chamber of Your grace, and draw this thirsty one of the desert of separation to the ocean of spiritual encounter. Make this ancient servant a companion of the assembly of Your favors, and enrich this poor, wretched one with true wealth.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Glory be to God.
O merciful Lord, preserve Thy friends in the stronghold of Thy protection, and rid them of the less and the more. Sever them from existence, possibility, and the corruptions of the worlds, and quicken and refresh them with the breaths of the placeless.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text: Guide Your servants, O my Lord, to the court of Your favor and bounty, and let them not be deprived of the wonders of Your grace and Your abundant blessings. For they know not what Thou hast destined for them by virtue of Thy mercy which encompasseth all creation. O Lord, outwardly they are weak and helpless, and inwardly they are but souls. Thou art the Most Bountiful, the Beneficent, the Most Exalted, the Greatest. O my God, let not Thy fierce wrath descend upon them, and cause them to wait until the time when the wonders of Thy mercy shall be revealed, so that they may turn unto Thee and ask forgiveness for all that they have done unto Thee. Verily, Thou art the Forgiving, the Most Merciful.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Recitation for Morning, Noon, and Evening
Whosoever desireth to supplicate, let him first wash his hands, and then recite:
O my God! Strengthen my hands that they may firmly grasp Thy Book, and withdraw them from all that is not Thine. Verily, Thou art the Almighty, the All-Bountiful.
Wash thy face, and recite:
O my Lord! I have turned my face towards Thee, illuminate it with the light of Thy countenance and protect it from the defilement of turning towards any other than Thee.
Then, let the suppliant arise and face the Qiblih (the point of adoration, that is, the House of Worship), and recite:
God beareth witness that there is none other God but He. His are the kingdoms of revelation and creation. Verily, He hath revealed Himself – the Source of revelation, He Who communed with God on Sinai, and Whose radiance hath illumined the horizons of the highest elevation, and the Tree of Utterance hath spoken in the realms of both heaven and earth, proclaiming unto all: 'Behold, the All-Possessing is come! He ruleth the heavens and the earth, the glory and the dominion. He is the Lord of all peoples, the Possessor of the Throne, both of heaven and earth!'
The suppliant shall bow down, place his hands on his knees, and then recite:
Exalted art Thou, O my God, beyond all praise, and above all description. None among Thy creation can sing Thy praise, nor can any one of them describe what Thou art.
The suppliant shall then arise, and opening his hands with palms upwards, let them fall upon his face, and recite:
O my God! Let him not be disappointed, who hath fixed his gaze upon the hem of Thy mercy. Thou art the Most Merciful of the merciful!
The suppliant shall then be seated, and recite:
I bear witness that Thou art God, and that Thou hast no partner. Thou hast revealed the Divine Word, and fulfilled Thy Covenant, and opened the door of Thy grace unto all who are in heaven and on earth. Entrust, O my God, those whom Thou hast drawn unto Thee, to the care of Thy loving-kindness. Though the world and all that is therein may change, yet shall they remain unchanged in their devotion to Thee. Verily, Thou art the Ever-Forgiving, the All-Bountiful.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O Glory of the Most Glorious

O servant of Truth, raise the hand of gratitude to the threshold of the One God and say: O Baha, my soul and my life be sacrificed for Thee, O my tender beloved, O my delightful companion! Grant grace that in Thy Most Glorious Kingdom I may gain entry with a radiant countenance and a soul like a garden of flowers and greenery, and strive in the service of Thy Cause, and scatter my life in Thy path, and become the dust of the path of Thy loved ones. Verily, Thou art the Bountiful, the Merciful.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O Lord! O Forgiver!
Grant this assembly confirmation and guidance, that it may illuminate the world with the light of unity. Enlighten the East and the West with the radiance of love and the light of concord. O Forgiving, Merciful One! Quicken hearts with the breaths of the Holy Spirit and make faces radiant like a candle, that they may illuminate the world and bestow mercy upon the souls. Thou art the Forgiver, Thou art the Bestower, and Thou art the Merciful.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is God
O Thou merciful Lord, guide the father of this loving one to the most exalted paradise, and forgive his sins and faults, that he may be reunited with the pure in the other world, his face radiant, accepted by the glance of divine favour. Thou art the Bountiful, the Merciful, the All-Forgiving, the Compassionate, the Kind.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is God.
O thou who art attentive to God, open thine eye to the Kingdom of the Unseen Majesty and begin this supplication with the request that: O Kind One, Thou cover and conceal; error from us, bestowal from Thee; cruelty from us, faithfulness from Thee; sin from us, refuge from Thee; sickness from us, healing from Thee. Cover and forgive, be faithful, give refuge, grant healing.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
God is Glory.
O Lord, enlighten this assembly and make this gathering a candle of the world, for their intention is the common good and their aim is service to humanity.
O Merciful Lord, such an assembly is deserving of Thy favours, and such a gathering is worthy of boundless grace and bestowal.
Thou art Almighty, Glorious, and All-Powerful, and Thou art the One, incomparable Lord.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

fa.cat: محافل روحانی مجامع و جلسات عمومی

prayers.text:
So generous, so full of grace, O Thou, Lord,
Who knowest the depths of my soul and my heart!

In the morning, He who consoles my soul, it is Thou, He who knows my deep aspiration, it is Thou.

The heart that has mentioned Thee even once
Will seek no balm but the ardent desire for Thee.

Withered be the heart that sighs not for Thee, Blind be the eyes if they weep not for Thee!

In hours of great sadness, O powerful Lord!
Remembering Thee illuminates my heart.

In Thy loving-kindness, breathe Thy spirit into me,
That what has never been may be forever.

Regard not our merit nor our worth,
But only Thy infinite grace, O generous Lord!

To these birds with broken wings and faltering flight,
Grant new wings, in Thy tender mercy.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O my Lord! O my God! These two bright moons have joined in wedlock for Thy love, are united for service at Thy sacred Threshold, and have become one soul for the work of Thy Cause.

O my Master, O Thou most merciful Lord! Make Thou this union a radiant strand of Thy boundless favour; O Thou bounteous, O Thou generous! Make them such a shining ray of Thy gifts, that from this great Tree may there spring forth such branches as may flourish and bear fruit in the rain of Thy bestowals.
Verily Thou art generous, verily Thou art almighty, verily Thou art merciful, O Thou most merciful!
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is the All-Glorious!

O Lord! Though I be a sinner, yet I am hopeful of Thy forgiveness. I am needy and poor, while Thou art the Precious and the Protector. I am in pain and lowly, while Thou art the remedy for every ailing one and captive. O God! Grant me Thy confirmation and bestow Thy grace, that I may strive in service, don the robe of bounty, and drink from the Salsabil (heavenly fountain) of Thy favor. And Glory be upon thee.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is God

O Forgiver of all sinners and wrongdoers, You are forgiving, You are bountiful, merciful. Look not at our merit or worthiness, but look upon Your grace and forgiveness. Immerse us in the ocean of Your forgiveness, and in the Most Glorious Paradise, make us companions with the Exalted Ones. Verily, Thou art the Merciful, the All-Bountiful.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is God

O Lord of all worlds! In this world, burn us with the fire of Thy love, and in the next, gladden us with the vision of Thy countenance.
In this world, melt us like a candle, and in the next, cause us to soar like a moth around the flame of Thy beauty.
Thou art my beloved, my joyous companion, and Thou art my auspicious, life-giving friend. And Glory be upon Thee.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is God!
O God, I am an innocent child. Protect me in Thy shelter, bestow Thy favor and grant Thy mercy. Educate me and nourish me with Thy bounty. Grant me capacity so that I may become heavenly, divine, spiritual, and illumined. Thou art the Mighty, the Powerful, and the Kind.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O Thou Holy Provider! Vouchsafe unto all the peoples of Bahá purity and holiness. Make them free from all uncleanness, and deliver them from all attachment. Save them from the commission of any defiling action, loosen them from the tie of every evil habit, that they may live in purity and freedom, good and clean, fit to serve at Thy Sacred Threshold and worthy to be connected with their Lord.

Deliver them from intoxicating drinks and tobacco; guard them, save them from this opium which causeth madness; cause them to savour the sweetness and fragrance of holiness, that they may drink their fill from the mystic cup of heavenly love and know the joy that draweth them nigh unto Thy Most Glorious Realm. For it is as Thou hast said: "All that thou hast in thy stores of wine will not satisfy the thirst of My love—bring unto me, O cupbearer, a cup of spiritual wine full as the ocean!"
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is God

O Lord, glorify him who is eager for Thy sight in the Kingdom, and place upon his head a crown of grace, and clothe him with a robe of supreme guidance. Grant him the tongue of eloquence and the power of clear expression; manifest the power of the Kingdom and the confirmation of the Holy Spirit.

Thou art the Mighty and the Powerful; Thou art the Bountiful, the Radiant, and the All-Seeing.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is God
O Unique Lord, make these souls wholly luminous, their hearts merciful, and their spirits divine, that they may in all stations arise to that which is befitting this Great Day, and be enabled to attain Thy pleasure.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

fa.cat: محافل روحانی مجامع و جلسات عمومی

prayers.text:
He is the Most Glorious

O my God, my soul be sacrificed for Thy loved ones, shed this depressed blood upon the dust in the path of Thy friends, and make this worn-out body dust upon the road and dust of the footsteps of Thy beloveds, O my God.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Huwal'Láh

O God, the darkness of strife, contention, and warfare amongst religions, peoples, and races hath veiled and obscured the horizon of truth. The world is in need of the light of guidance. Therefore, O Lord, bestow Thy favour that the Sun of Truth may illuminate the world from horizon to horizon.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Huwa'lláh
O Lord, make these wanderers of Thy court lovers, detached from stranger and self. Thou art the Capable and the Mighty.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

fa.cat: راز و نياز- شكر و ستايش

prayers.text:
The friends of God ... should give as much as they are able, however modest their contribution may be. God does not burden any soul beyond its capacity. Such donations must come from everywhere and from every believer ... O friends of God! Be assured that for these donations, your agriculture, your trade, and your commerce will be blessed with manifold growth and abundant gifts. Whoever performs a good deed will receive tenfold reward. Without a doubt, the living God will abundantly confirm those who sacrifice their wealth in His path.
O God, my God! Enlighten the faces of those who truly love Thee, and assist them with the victorious army of Thine angels. Set their steps securely on Thy straight path and open to them, through Thine eternal bounty, the gates of Thy blessings; for on Thy path, for the protection of Thy faith, they spend of that which Thou hast bestowed upon them. In Thy remembrance they place their trust, out of love for Thee they give their hearts, and in prayer to Thy beauty, seeking Thy favour, they withhold not what they possess.
O my Lord! Appoint for them an abundant share, a precise recompense, a secure reward!
Verily, Thou art the Sustainer, the Helper, the Bountiful, the Generous, the Ever-Giver.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O my Lord, my God, remove Thou my sorrow with Thy bounteous favour, and dispel my grief with Thy sovereignty and Thy might. O my Lord, my God, Thou seest

that I have turned unto Thee when sorrow hath encompassed me on every side;    O Thou, the Lord of all life, and above all names and all things, I implore Thee by Thy name that doth inflame the hearts and souls of men, by the waves of Thy mercy, and the splendours of the sun of Thy favour, count me

amongst them that turn unto Thee, O Thou, the Lord of all names, and the Fashioner of the heavens!

    O my Lord, Thou seest what hath befallen me in Thy days, and I implore Thee by the Dhikr of Thy name and the mysteries of Thy attributes, to ordain for me that which will cause me to arise to serve Thee and to laud Thy praises. Verily, Thou art the All-Bountiful, the Most High, the Hearer of all prayers!

    And lastly, I beseech Thee by the light of Thy countenance, to bless my deeds, to redeem my debts, and to remove my straits. Thou art He by Whom all do affirm Thy power and Thy dominion, and all hearts that know Thee bear witness to Thy sovereignty and Thy sovereignty. There is none other God besides Thee, the Hearer and the Answerer.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O Lord! My Master! I express gratitude to Thee for the favor Thou hast bestowed upon Thy devoted handmaid.
This Thy handmaid prays and beseeches Thee, for verily Thou hast guided her toward Thy visible dominion, made her worthy to hear Thy call in this fleeting world, and shown her the signs that attest to Thy victorious reign over all substances.
O Lord! That which is in my womb, I dedicate to Thee. With Thy grace and generosity, make him a praiseworthy and fortunate child in Thy kingdom, so that he may flourish and develop under Thy teachings. Verily Thou art merciful, verily Thou art the Lord of great favors.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

ja.cat: 「聖なる計画の書簡」のティーチングの祈り

prayers.text: O Lord! Make this youth a shining candle, and this creation a flourishing tree. Bestow upon him the knowledge of Thee, and fill him with new strength at each sunrise; guard him within the garden of Thy good pleasure, that he may be saved from error, dedicating himself to service in Thy cause, a guide to the erring, a comforter of the afflicted, a deliverer of the captives, and a bringer of gladness to the weary, that all may be blessed by remembering Thee and praising Thee. Thou art the Almighty, the All-Possessing.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text: By the strength of Thy name nothing shall ever wound me, and by Thy love all the afflictions of this world shall never harm me.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text: ou, almighty Lord! We are all under the formidable grasp of Thy power. Thou art our support and our help. Grant us Thy tender mercy and Thy generosity, open the doors of grace and cast upon us the gaze of Thy favors. May a life-giving breeze blow upon us and revive our languishing hearts. Enlighten our eyes and make the sanctuary of our heart the envy of all flowering gardens. Delight each soul, gladden each spirit. Reveal Thy ancient power and manifest Thy great might. Cause the birds of human souls to soar to new heights and permit Thy confidants in this world below to comprehend the mysteries of Thy kingdom. Strengthen our steps and grant us an unwavering heart. We are sinners, and Thou art the Ever-Forgiving. We are Thy servants, and Thou art the Sovereign Lord. We wander homeless, and Thou art our haven and our refuge. By Thy grace, assist us, help us to spread Thy sweet fragrances and to exalt Thy word. Elevate the rank of the dispossessed and grant Thy inexhaustible treasure to the destitute. Give Thy strength to the weak and bestow heavenly power upon the vulnerable. Thou art the Benefactor, Thou art the Bountiful, Thou art the Lord who reigneth over all things.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Alláh'u'Abhá!
O my pure Lord and merciful God! Grant such power that we may contend with all the diverse obstacles of the earth, and bestow such might that we may, like the boundless ocean, surge upon the shores of east and west.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O Lord! We are weak; give us strength. O Lord! We are ignorant; give us knowledge. O Master! We are poor; make us wealthy. O God! We are lifeless; give us consciousness. O Controller of the Universe! We are the embodiment of inferiority; give us glory in Your kingdom.

O Lord of the Universe! If You help us, we too will become bright stars; if You do not help, we will go to ruin. O Master! Make us strong. O Supreme God! Grant us victory. O Supreme Soul! Help us to conquer our ego and keep our desires under control. O Father! Grant us liberation from the bonds of the material world.

O Lord! May we be committed to Your service, be absorbed in Your devotion, and sincerely strive to reach Your kingdom; for that, infuse Your holy life-force and give us consciousness. O Master! You are almighty. O God! You are forgiving. O Lord! You are an ocean of compassion.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
China! China! China! Go to China! The Cause of Baháʼu'lláh must reach China. Where are the holy Bahá'ís who go to China to teach? China has the greatest potential. The hearts of the Chinese people are most pure and most eager to seek truth. To teach the Chinese, one must first possess their spirit, study their scriptures, understand their customs, and speak to them from their perspective, in their language. The teacher must always remember the spiritual well-being of the Chinese people and have no selfish motives. In China, one person can teach many, can nurture outstanding individuals with divine character, who can become lamps to illumine the world of humanity. I verily say, the Chinese people do not love any form of deceit and hypocrisy; they are inspired by noble ideals. Regrettably, my physical condition does not permit, otherwise I would personally go to China.
China is a country of the future. I hope that suitable teachers will be inspired to go to that vast country, lay the foundation of the Kingdom of God, promote the essence of divine civilization, and raise the banner of the Cause of Baháʼu'lláh to invite people to God's sacred feast.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O God, refresh and gladden my spirit. Purify my heart. Enlighten my powers. Into Thy hands I commit all my concerns. Thou art my Guide and my Refuge. Let not sadness and anxiety possess me, but rather contentment and joy.

O God, Thou art more my Friend than I am to myself. I dedicate myself to Thee, O Lord.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

th.cat: บทอธิษฐานเพื่อพัฒนาจิตใจ

prayers.text: He is God! O Lord! How shall we thank Thee! Thy bounties are limitless, and our gratitude but limited. How can the limited render thanks to the limitless? Incapable are we of offering thanks for Thy mercies. Utterly powerless, we turn unto Thy Kingdom, and beg Thee to increase Thy bestowal and bounty. Thou art the Giver, Thou art the Bestower, Thou art the Powerful.
prayers.name: Grace at meals (How shall we thank Thee)
prayers.notes: Recorded utterance; cited from HM Balyuzi, Abdul-Bahá the Centre of the Covenant, p. 307

prayers.text: O Lord! Help all the people of the earth to enter the paradise of Thy religion, so that no soul brought into existence may remain outside the bounds of Thy favour. From eternity, Thou hast been able to do that which is pleasing unto Thee and that which is Thy will.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Glory be to Thee, O Lord, my God! I implore Thee by Thy Name by which He Who is Thy Beauty was established upon the throne of Thy Cause, Thy Name by which Thou dost change and gather together all things, and call them to account, and recompense them, and preserve them all, and sustain them all, ‒ I implore Thee to protect this handmaiden who hath sought refuge in Thee, sought the succour of Him in Whom Thou hast revealed Thyself, and hath fully trusted and relied upon Thee.
She is ailing, O my God, and hath entered into the shadow of Thy Tree of Healing; suffering she hath found sanctuary in Thy City of Refuge; sick she hath sought Thy Fount of Favours; distressed she hath hastened to attain Thy River of Peace; burdened with sins she hath turned her face toward the court of Thy forgiveness.
Bestow on her, by reason of Thy supreme dominion and gracious favour, O my God and my Beloved, the raiment of Thy comfort and healing, and make her to drink of the cup of Thy loving-kindness and Thy favours. Protect her also from all distress and tribulation, from all pain and sickness, from whatsoever may be displeasing unto Thee.
Thou art indeed most exalted above all, save Thyself. Thou art indeed the Healer, the Bestower, the Protector, the Forgiver, the Most Merciful.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

th.cat: บทอธิษฐานขอความคุ้มครอง

prayers.text: Praise be to God Who existed ere all things were brought into being, and with Whom nothing can be compared. He was manifest when there was none other to contemplate His reality. He is the One Whose essence the hearts of the knowing have longed to fathom, yet fail utterly to apprehend even the most infinitesimal glimpse of His attributes [4o]... …The glory of glories and the most resplendent light rest upon Thee, O my God. Thy majesty is so transcendent… O my God, my Lord and my Master! I have detached myself… I adjure Thee by Thy might, O my God!... O My God, O my Lord, O my Master! I beg Thee to forgive me
prayers.notes: Base code entry for FK; sub-codes contain actual prayers

prayers.text:
PROTECTION

In the Name of God, the King whose shelter is beyond beauty and magnificence, the Almighty.

The King reigns, in Whose hand is the beginning of dominion. He creates anything He willeth from His command, "Be," and it is. He is the power of rule until now, and will continue thus until times to come. He makes victorious whomever He willeth, by the power of His desire. He is indeed the Powerful, the Almighty. Above Him is all beauty and a shelter beyond all the dominions of Manifestation and the

Creation and all that abides between them. He is indeed the Powerful, the All-Powerful. From eternity He is the beginning of dominion, which cannot be comprehended and will continue thus until eternity. And He is indeed the Lord of might and power. All the worlds in heaven and earth and all that is between them are God's, and His power is above all things. All that is precious in the earth and the heavens and all that is between them belongs to Him, and His protection abides over all things. He is the Deliverer of the heavens and the earth and all that is between them, and He is indeed the Witness over all things. He is the Lord of Judgment for all those in the heavens and the earth and all that is between them, and indeed God is the Judge who does not err. He directs the distribution of things to all people in the heavens and the earth and all that is between them. He is indeed the Supreme Protector in the Kingdom. In His Hands are the keys of the heavens and the earth and all that is between

them. From His grace He bestows gifts, from the power of His command. Indeed His mercy encompasses all things, and He is the All-Knowing.

Say: God is sufficient for me; And He is the One in Whose hands are the dominions of all things. From the power of His might...
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
The Protector

In the Name of God, the Lord, transcendent is peace and beauty, the All-Encompassing Essence.

The Lord Who holds in His hand the beginning of sovereignty. He createth whatsoever He willeth by His command: 'Be,' and it is. And He is the power of authority until now, and will continue so until times hereafter. He maketh victorious whomever He willeth by the power of His will. And He is indeed the All-Powerful, the All-Sovereign.

Above Him is all beauty and peace transcendent in all the dominions of Revelation and Creation and whatsoever is within their expanse. Verily He is the All-Power, the All-Might. From eternity He is the Beginning of power, incomprehensible, and will continue so unto eternity. And He is indeed the Lord of majesty and power.

All the worlds in heaven and earth and whatsoever is within their expanse belong to God, and His power is above all things. All precious things on earth and in heaven and all things within their expanse are His, and His protection is over all things. And He is the Deliverer of heaven and earth and whatsoever is within their expanse, and verily He is the Witness over all things.

And He is the Lord of Judgment for all those in heaven and earth and whatsoever is within their expanse, and verily God is not unjust in judging. He ordains the division of things unto all in heaven and earth and whatsoever is within their expanse. Verily He is the Supreme Protector in Sovereignty. In His hand are the keys of heaven and earth and whatsoever is within their expanse.

From His favour He bestoweth gifts, from the power of His command. Verily His bounty encompasses all things, and He is the All-Knowing.

Say: God is sufficient unto me; and He it is Who in His hands holdeth the dominion of all things. By the power of His...
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
(Leaving the house)

     O my Lord, my God! I have left my house, holding fast to the cords of Thy love, and committed my affairs to Thy care and protection. I implore Thy favour to protect me from all who are seeking to bring me to ruin, and from the unjust, the wicked, and the ungodly, who have strayed far from Thee. Let Thy succour guard me, and Thy divine power preserve me, that I may return to my home in safety.

Verily, Thou art the All-Bounteous, the Helper in distress, the Self-Subsisting.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text: In the name of God, the One, the Peerless. In the Name of God, the One, the Peerless. In the Name of God, the Singular, the Matchless. In the Name of God, the Singular, the Matchless. In the Name of God, the Unique, the Supreme. In the Name of God, the Singular, the Matchless [4o]

O, my adoring God, all glory and majesty, all dominion and light, beauty and radiance belong to You. To whomever You will, You grant dominion. From whomever You will, You take it away. The Possessor of all, the Most High above all, there is no God but You. You bring into existence the entire universe and all souls within it from nothingness. There is nothing comparable to You – all besides You is unworthy before Your holy presence and the glory of Your Essence.

In Your great Book, You Yourself said: “Eyes cannot perceive Him, but He perceives eyes. He is the All-Perceiving, the All-Knowing.” I cannot praise Your perfections better than what You have said. May Your glory increase, O my God. Verily, no matter how keen the eye or sharp the intellect, it lacks the capacity to comprehend the nature of even Your humblest sign. Verily, You are the true God, there is no God beside You. Only You alone can manifest Your attributes; no praise from anyone besides You can reach Your holy court, and I bear witness that no one besides You can comprehend Your qualities.

Glory be to You – You are exalted beyond any description by anyone besides You, because the comprehension of humankind is insufficient to praise Your worthiness at a suitable level or to understand the deep nature of Your Essence. None of Your creatures besides You has the power to describe Your glory or fully apprehend it. I have come to know You, O my adoring God, because You Yourself have revealed Yourself to me; had You not revealed Yourself, I would never have known You. I worship You because You have called me; had it not been for Your call, I would not have been able to worship You. O my adoring God, You are glorious; my faults are heavy and my sins are great. What a shameful fate I face before Your holy throne. I have not known You to the extent You have revealed Yourself to me; I have not worshipped You with the devotion that befits Your call; I have not submitted to You at a level worthy of You, because I have not walked in the path of Your love in the manner You have inspired.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Praised be Thou, O God, for Thy revelation of love to mankind! O Thou, who art our life and light, lead Thy servants on Thy path and make us rich in Thee and free from all except Thee.

O God, teach us of Thy unity and grant us understanding of Thy incomparable station, so that we may not see anyone other than Thee. Thou art the Merciful and the bestower of grace!

O God, enkindle in the hearts of Thy loved ones the fire of Thy love, so that it may consume the thought of all that is not of Thee.

O God, reveal to us Thy exalted eternity - that Thou hast always been and shall always remain, and that no God exists except Thee. Verily, in Thee we find safety and strength.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
You are God, and there is no God other than You.

    O my God! Pure and Holy are You, O my God! Verily, You are the Most Capable in this world. You are the Almighty of all beings, and the creation, the whole of it, is in Your power. You create whatsoever You will. There is no wise and exalted God other than You.

    O my God! Pure and Holy are You, O my God! Following those who stand in Your presence, following those who attain Your countenance, I ask of You: Look upon us with a forgiving eye, cast upon us Your special attention. Ignite us with the fire of Your love and let us drink from the water of Your grace. Then give us strength to be steadfast in the path of love and affection, and grant us a dwelling-place in Your holy presence, for You are the Generous, the Forgiving, the Knowing, and the Wise.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
In His Name, the Most Glorious, the Most High, the All-Glorious.

Praise be to Thee, O Lord my God! O Thou Who art my God, my Master, my Lord, my Helper, my Hope, my Refuge, and my Light.

I beseech Thee, through Thy Hidden and Treasured Name, which no one knoweth except Thyself, to protect the bearer of this Tablet from all calamities and pestilential diseases, and from every wicked person, whether male or female; from the evil wrought by the makers of mischief, and from the plots of the unbelievers.

Ward off from him also, O my God, all pain and suffering, O Thou Who holdest in Thy hand the dominion of all things. Thou art, indeed, the Almighty over all things. Thou doest whatsoever Thou wishest, and commandest as Thou pleasest.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text: It is incumbent upon you to cherish them with the utmost affection and loving-kindness, and to encourage them to spiritual pursuits, that their faces may be turned towards God, that their lives may be molded after the pattern of His laws and their character be unsurpassed; that they may possess all praiseworthy and noble attributes; that they may acquire the knowledge of all sciences and arts; that they may be consecrated to the spiritual and eternal life, be enkindled with the fire of the love of God, and be numbered among the spiritual inhabitants of His Kingdom, receiving from the Heavenly Host the education that is of God.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Glorified be Thy Name, O Lord! In whom shall I seek refuge, since Thou art, in truth, my God and my Beloved; to whom shall I turn for shelter, since Thou art my Lord and my Possessor; and to whom shall I flee, since Thou art, in truth, my Master and my Sanctuary; and to whom shall I implore, since Thou art, in truth, my Treasure and the Object of my desire; and through whom shall I plead before Thee, since Thou art, in truth, my highest aspiration and my supreme desire? All hope is frustrated, save the yearning for Thy celestial grace, and every door is shut, save that which leadeth to the Fount of Thy blessings.

O my Lord, I beseech Thee by the effulgence of Thy most resplendent glory, before Whose radiance all souls humbly bow down and prostrate in adoration for love of Thee – a glory that doth so radiate that fire is converted into light, the dead are quickened, and all difficulty is transformed into ease. I implore Thee by that great, that wondrous glory and by the glory of Thy exalted sovereignty, O Thou Who art the Lord of indomitable power, that through Thy bounty Thou mayest transform us into that which is possessed by Thee, and mayest help us to become the sources of Thy light, and mayest graciously bestow upon us that which is worthy of the majesty of Thy transcendent dominion. For unto Thee have I lifted up my hands, O Lord, and in Thee have I found the support that sustaineth me, O Lord, and unto Thee have I resigned myself, O Lord, and in Thee have I placed all my trust, O Lord, and by Thee am I strengthened, O Lord.
Verily, there is no power, nor strength, except in Thee.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Ô my Lord, since Thou holdest the measure of all things, ordain for me and for those who believe in Thee what Thou deemest best for us, according to what the Mother-Book prescribeth.
Thy bounteous favours descend ceaselessly upon those who cherish Thy love. They who recognize Thy divine unity receive abundantly the wondrous signs of Thy celestial bounty. All that Thou destinest for us is in Thy hands, we implore Thee to grant us all the good that is within Thy knowledge.
O my Lord, protect me from all the ills perceived by Thy omniscience, for there is no power nor strength save in Thee, nor is any victory possible save from Thy presence; to Thee alone appertaineth command. All that God willeth is accomplished, all that He willeth not is not accomplished.
There is no power, no force save in God, the Supreme, the Almighty.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

th.cat: บทอธิษฐานขอความคุ้มครอง

prayers.text: Praised be Thou, O Lord. Forgive us our transgressions, have mercy upon us and guide us back unto Thee. Grant that we may not rely on any other save Thee, and send down upon us, through Thy bounty, that which Thou dost love and desire and is meet for Thee. Exalt the spiritual state of them that truly believe, and forgive them through Thy tender forgiveness. Verily, Thou art the Help in Great Distress, the Self-Subsistent.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

th.cat: บทอธิษฐานเพื่อการอภัย

prayers.text: I have awakened in Thy shelter, O my God, and it behooves him who seeketh this shelter to abide within the tabernacle of Thy protection and the fortress of Thine invincibility.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text: In this day the rustling of the Divine Lote-Tree hath been raised up, and the Sun of Utterance hath dawned and shone forth from the horizon of the heaven of inner meanings, and the Path hath been made manifest [3.5s]... ...The days of the Manifestations of God have, in one sense, been referred to as the Day of God. Yet these days, as they are mentioned in all the Holy Books… How can I choose to sleep, O God, my God, when the eyes of them that long for Thee are wakeful... I have risen this morning by Thy grace, O my God, and left my home trusting wholly in Thee... This, Thy servant, seeketh to sleep in the shelter of Thy mercy... I have wakened in Thy shelter, O my God
prayers.notes: Base code entry for FK; sub-codes contain actual prayers

prayers.text:
Prayer for a Husband

O Lord, my Lord! Thy handmaid who is before Thee supplicates Thee, trusteth in Thee, turneth towards Thee, and calleth upon Thee. Bestow upon him Thy favour, reveal unto him the mysteries of Thy Faith, and the glory of Thy sovereignty.

O Lord! Enable my husband to become enlightened, gladden his heart with the light of Thy knowledge which leads unto Thee, and draw his soul unto the beauty of Thy countenance.

O Lord! Remove the veils which hide him from Thine eyes, pour down upon him Thy plenteous blessings, that he may be intoxicated with the wine of love for Thee. Make him one of the heavenly hosts that tread this earthly realm, as if his spirit were soaring in the firmament of heaven; make him a radiant star shining with the light of Thine omnipotence amongst Thy people.

Verily, Thou art the All-Bounteous, the Ever-Giving, the Succourer.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text: Praise be unto God, incomparable in majesty, power and beauty, peerless in glory, might and grandeur… I magnify Thy Name, O my God, and offer thanksgiving
prayers.notes: Base code entry for FK; sub-codes contain actual prayers

prayers.text:
Praise be to Thy Name, O my God. Lord of all, my refuge, the refuge of all, my Beloved, the Beloved of all, my Strength, the Strength of all, my King, the King of all, my Sovereign, the Sovereign of all, my Goal, the Goal of all. Thou art He Who remindeth me, Who remindeth all. I beg of Thee, O my God, suffer me not to be far from the ocean of Thy mercy, and protect me with that which will keep me distant from Thy shores.

Nothing can profit me except Thee, O my God. Every life that is not near Thee is of no avail to me. I beg of Thee, O my Lord, through the abundance of Thy riches which have made Thee independent of all save Thyself, make me to be among those who have turned their faces unto Thee, and have arisen to worship Thee.

O my Lord, forgive Thy servants, men and women. Thou art indeed the Ever-Forgiving, the Compassionate.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
It is incumbent upon the servants of God, after every Prayer, to ask God to forgive their parents. Then shall God announce: 'Thousands of what you have asked for your parents shall be your reward'." - Báb

O God! Thou seest the hands of our supplication lifted up unto the heaven of Thy grace and Thy bounty. Let them be filled with the treasures of Thy boundless generosity and mercy. Forgive us and our fathers and our mothers, and grant whatsoever we desire from the ocean of Thy mercy and the wellspring of Thy divine generosity. Accept, O Beloved of our hearts, all our works in Thy path. Verily, Thou art the All-Powerful, the Most High, the Incomparable, the One, the All-Forgiving, the Most Merciful.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text: Praised be God Who hath manifested through the influence of His Most Exalted Pen unto all who dwell in the kingdom of creation, whereupon every weary one hath spoken [3.5s]... ...Let not the happenings of the world sadden you. I swear by God! The sea of joy yearneth… Blessed is the spot, and the house… Regarding what ye had written as to the Mashriqu’l-Adhkár’s having been established… It is the binding command of God that in every locality whatever hath been or will be made available for the Ḥuqúqu’lláh should be submitted to His Holy Presence
prayers.notes: Base code entry for FK; sub-codes contain actual prayers

prayers.text:
Blessed is he who reflects in his heart on that which hath been revealed in the Books of God, the Helper in Danger, the Self-Subsistent.

Blessed are they who obey the commandments of God; blessed are they who have known His Truth; blessed are they who judge all things with justice and hold fast to My unbreakable Covenant of Justice.

Blessed is he who standeth firm in this Covenant and refuseth to turn aside from His truth in the days when trials encompass the whole world.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O Lord, my God, praised be Thou! I give thanks unto Thee as Thy Bounty hath called me to life in Thy days, and breathed into me Thy love and Thy wisdom. I implore Thee by Thy Name by which the goodly pearls of Thy wisdom and Thy revelation have been drawn out from the treasuries of the hearts of Thy servants, as they draw nigh unto Thee, and as the Morning Star of Thy Most Bountiful Name hath caused to shine forth, its brilliance upon all that is in Thy heaven and all that is on Thy earth, that I may be nourished by Thy grace and Thy gifts, with Thy wondrous bestowals and Thy boundless favour.

      O my God, these are the first days of my life, which have been consecrated by Thy Bounty and by Thyself. Since Thou hast poured out upon me so great a glory, withhold not from me that which Thou hast ordained for Thy chosen ones.

      O my God, I am but a humble seed which Thy Bounty hath sown in the soil of Thy love, and caused to grow in the day of Thy favour. I therefore implore Thee by my true self, by the waters of Thy tender mercy and the living springs of Thy grace. Send down upon it from the heaven of Thy loving-kindness, that which shall make it to flourish and to grow beneath Thy shadow and within the courts of Thy sanctuary. Thou art He who watereth with the water of the hearts of all who know Thee from Thy great river and the springs of Thy living waters.

       Praised be God, the Lord of worlds.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text: Praised be to Thee, O God, my God! I beg of Thee, by Thy name the Enduring One by which the Manifestations of Thy Cause… This is, O my God, the first of the days on which Thou hast bidden Thy loved ones observe the Fast
prayers.notes: Base code entry for FK; sub-codes contain actual prayers

prayers.text:
O Lord, for them that are near unto Thee, Thy trials are the healing remedies, and for all who love Thee Thy sword the object of desire, and for them that yearn for Thee Thy lances their cherished hope, and for them that know Thy truth Thy command the sole hope!

By Thy holy sweetness, and the radiance of the glory of Thy countenance, I implore Thee, send down upon us from Thy holy habitation which is in the highest heavens that which will draw us nigh unto Thee.

O my God, make firm our feet within Thy Cause, illuminate our hearts with the light of Thy knowledge, and brighten our breasts with the radiance of Thy name.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is the Most Holy, the Most Glorious.

In the name of God, the Compasionate, the Merciful! Praise be to God, the Lord of all worlds!

O Thou my God, my Refuge and my Shelter! How can I fitly call upon Thee, when even the most wondrous words of glorification and the most eloquent praises are inadequate, O Thou Almighty and Forgiving, knowing that the tongue of every eloquent speaker stammereth, and every eulogy from human pen or tongue becometh confused in its endeavour to extol even one of the signs of Thy almighty power or to glorify a single Word which hath been created by Thee. Verily the wings of the birds of human intellect are broken in their attempt to soar to the spheres of Thy divine holiness, and the spiders of vain fancy are unable to weave their fragile web upon the loftiest pinnacles of the canopy of Thy knowledge. Thus there is no recourse for me but to acknowledge my powerlessness and shortcomings, and there is no abode for me save in the depths of poverty and destitution. Verily, powerlessness to comprehend Thee is the essence of comprehension, confession of shortcomings is the only means of reaching Thy presence, and acknowledgment of poverty is the source of true riches.

O Lord! Graciously assist me and Thy devoted servants in our servitude to Thy Exalted Threshold, strengthen us in our supplications to Thy divine holiness, and enable us to be humble and submissive at the door of Thy oneness. Make my steps powerful in Thy path, O my Lord, and enlighten my heart with the rays that shine from the heaven of Thy mysteries. Quicken my spirit with the animating breeze diffused from the paradise of Thy forgiveness and pardon, and gladden my soul through the refreshing breath conveyed from the meads of Thy holiness. Verl
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

nl.cat: Gebeden, vertaald ter gelegenheid van de honderdste gedenkdag in 2021van het heengaan van de Meester

prayers.text: This is an Epistle which the All-Merciful hath sent down from the Kingdom of Utterance. It is truly a breath of life… Say: Magnified be Thy Name, O Lord my God!
prayers.notes: Base code entry for FK; sub-codes contain actual prayers

prayers.text:
* Selections from the Tablet of Wisdom
# In the Name of the Creative, the All-Knowing, the Wise
A Book which the Most Merciful hath sent down from the realm of Revelation, and which is the spirit of life unto them that are in the world. Exalted be God, the Lord of all worlds.
Wherein he that remembereth God, his Lord, doth remember Him; verily it is the Most Noble in a Great Tablet.
O Muhammad! Hear the summons from the centre of glory, from the Lote-tree that is raised on saffron plains: Verily, there is none other God but Me, the All-Knowing, the All-Wise.
Be as the breezes of the Most Merciful unto the trees of existence and their nurturer, in the name of thy Lord, the Just, the All-Informed.
We desired to remind thee of that whereby men may be reminded, that they may relinquish what is with them and turn their faces toward God, the Lord of the sincere.

We do counsel the servants in these days wherein the face of justice is obscured, and the cheek of ignorance hath become radiant, and the veil of reason is rent asunder, and rest and faithfulness have vanished, and tribulation and affliction have overflowed; and in these days wherein covenants have been broken and agreements disregarded, no soul knoweth what doth guide him or what doth lead him astray.

Say: O people, relinquish ye vices and embrace virtues.
Be a good example unto men, and a page wherein they may be reminded.
Whosoever riseth to serve the Cause, it behoveth him to proclaim wisdom and strive to remove ignorance from amongst mankind.
Say: Unite in your word, and be of one accord in your opinion, and make your radiance better than your twilight, and your morrow better than your yesterday.
Man's preferment lieth in service and perfection, not in adornment, nor in riches and wealth.
Let your words be purified from all vain desires, and your deeds cleansed from hypocrisy.
Say: Spend not the precious moments of your lives in the gratification of your lower selves, nor limit your concerns to your own personal benefit.
Give when ye have, and be patient when ye have not; for verily, after every hardship cometh ease, and with every distress there is relief.
Avoid idleness and sloth, and cling to that which profiteth the world, whether it be small or great.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O God my God! Thou seest me standing before the door of Thy forgiveness and benevolence, turning my gaze toward the horizon of Thy bountiful favours and manifold blessings. I beg of Thee by Thy sweet accents and by the shrill voice of Thy Pen, O Lord of all mankind, to graciously aid Thy servants as it befitteth Thy days and beseemeth the glory of Thy manifestation and Thy majesty. Verily potent art Thou to do whatsoever Thou willest. All they that dwell in the heavens and on the earth bear witness to Thy power and Thy might, to Thy glory and Thy bounteousness. Praise be to Thee, O Lord of the worlds and the Well-Beloved of the heart of every man of understanding!

Thou Beholdest, O myGod, the essence of poverty seeking the ocean of Thy wealth and the substance of iniquity yearning for the waters of Thy forgiveness and Thy tender mercy. Grant Thou, O my God, that which beseemeth Thy great glory and befitteth the loftiness of Thy boundless grace. Thou art in truth the All-Bountiful, the Lord of grace abounding, the Ordainer, the All-Wise. No God is there but Thee, the Most Powerful, the All-Compelling, the Omnipotent.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Exalted art Thou, O Lord my God! I beseech Thee by those whom Thou hast bidden to observe the Fast for the sake of Thy love and good-pleasure, who have demonstrated their allegiance to Thy law and followed Thy verses and precepts, and who have broken their fast while enjoying near access to Thee and beholding Thy countenance. By Thy glory! Since they are turning to the court of Thy good-pleasure, all their days are days of fasting. Were the mouth of Thy will to address them saying: “Observe, for My beauty's sake, the fast, O people, and set no limit to its duration,” I swear by the majesty of Thy glory, that every one of them will faithfully observe it, will abstain from whatsoever will violate Thy law, and will continue to do so until they yield up their souls unto Thee, for they have tasted the sweetness of Thy call, and become inebriated with Thy remembrance and praise and with the words proceeding from the lips of Thy command.

I beseech Thee, O Lord, by Thyself, the Exalted, the Most High, and by Thy Latter Manifestation through Whom the kingdom of names and the dominion of attributes have been convulsed, and the inhabitants of earth and heaven became intoxicated, and all who dwell in the realms of Revelation and creation trembled except such as have fasted from all that is repugnant to Thy good-pleasure and restrained themselves from turning toward aught besides Thee, to include us among them and to write down our names on the Tablet whereon Thou hast inscribed their names. O God, through the wonders of Thy might and the tokens of Thy sovereignty and grandeur, Thou didst send forth their names from out of the sea of Thy names, and didst create their inner essences out of the substance of Thy love, and their inmost beings from the spirit of Thy Cause. Theirs is a reunion not succeeded by separation, a nearness that knoweth no remoteness, and a perpetuity that hath no end. Verily, these are servants who ever recount Thee, who eternally circle round Thee, and who circumambulate the sanctuary of Thy presence and the Kaaba of reunion with Thee.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text: "I beg Thee to forgive me, O my Lord, for every mention but the mention of Thee, and for every praise but the praise of Thee, and for every delight but delight in Thy nearness, and for every pleasure but the pleasure of communion with Thee, and for every joy but the joy of Thy love and of Thy good-pleasure, and for all things pertaining unto me which bear no relationship unto Thee, O Thou Who art the Lord of lords, He Who provideth the means and unlocketh the doors."

O my God, my Master, the Object of my longing. Thy servant wishes to sleep in the shelter of Thy mercy and rest in the tent of Thy grace. He supplicates Thee, that Thou mayest watch over him and that naught may harm him.

I beseech Thee, O my Lord, by Thy Eye, that sleepeth not, protect my eyes, that they may look upon naught but Thee. Make then their gaze keen, that they may discern Thy signs and behold the horizon of Thy Revelation. Thou art He, before Whose manifestations of highest power all power trembles in its very essence.

There is no God but Thou, the Almighty, the All-Subduing, the All-Perfect.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
The Huqúqu'lláh is verily a weighty law. All men are under obligation to offer it, for it is the source of grace and abundance and all good. It is a generous gift from God, the Possessor of all, Who bestoweth freely in all His worlds, unto every soul. -Baháʼu'lláh

I beseech Thee, O my Lord, that Thou mayest confirm him.

O Thou Who art the Lord of all creation, and the Goal of all that hath been created, O my Lord, praise be unto Thee. By the inner and outer tongue I bear witness that Thou hast manifested Thyself and revealed Thy signs, hast sent down Thy proofs, and proclaimed Thy teachings. I bear witness that Thou art self-sufficing, needing none other than Thyself, and that Thou art sanctified above all that hath been created. By the transcendent glory of Thy Cause and the supreme power of Thy Word, I beseech Thee to confirm him. He is one who longeth to offer what Thou hast ordained in Thy Book, and by his observance of what is incumbent upon him, to enable the sweet savours of Thy acceptance to be diffused. Verily, Thou art the Most Powerful, the Most Gracious, the All-Forgiving, the Most Generous.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Naw-Rúz - "O Pen of Glory, say: We have enjoined upon you fasting for a fixed number of days (nineteen), and when this is ended, We have designated Naw-Rúz (the first day of the New Year) as a festival for you." - Kitáb-i-Aqdas.

Thou art greatly praised, O my Lord, since Thou hast ordained Naw-Rúz as a festival for those who have observed the Fast for love of Thee and abstained from that which is abhorrent in Thy sight. Ordain this, O my Supreme Lord, so that the fire of Thy love and the warmth generated by the Fast enjoined by Thee may inspire them in Thy Faith and engage them in Thy praise and remembrance.

Since Thou hast adorned them, O my Supreme Lord, with the adornment of the Fast as directed by Thee, therefore, do Thou also adorn them with the adornment of Thy approval through Thy compassion and gracious bounty. For the deeds of men are wholly dependent on Thy pleasure and subject to Thy command. Him who breaketh the Fast, Thou wilt regard as him who hath observed the Fast, and count him among those who have observed the Fast from eternity. Whosoever hath observed the Fast and afterwards hath broken it, thy command shall be thus: that such a person be reckoned among them who have defiled the raiment of Thy revealed Word with dust and have been removed far from the pure waters of this living, heavenly fountain.

Thou art He through Whom the banner of 'Praise be to Thee in Thy works' hath been raised, and the standard of 'Thy command is fulfilled' hath been unfurled. Make known this Thy station, O my Lord, unto Thy servants, that they may be aware that the excellence of all things dependeth on Thy command and Thy Word, and the virtue of every deed on the pleasure of Thy good-pleasure and Thy will, and that they may know that the reins of men's actions are within the grasp of Thy approval and command. Make them aware that this day
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

th.cat: บทอธิษฐานเมื่อประสบความยากลำบาก

prayers.text:
Long Healing

He is the Healer, the Sufficer, the Helper, the All-Merciful. I call upon Thee, O Glorified, O Faithful, O Glorious One! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider! I call upon Thee, O Lord, O Abider! I call upon Thee, O Lord, O Peerless, O Eternal, O Unique One! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider!

	I call upon Thee, O Lord, O Most Praised, O Holy, O Helper! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider! I call upon Thee, O Lord, O Peerless, O Eternal, O Unique One! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider!

	I call upon Thee, O Lord, O Most Praised, O Holy, O Helper! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider!

	I call upon Thee, O Lord, O All-Knowing, O All-Wise, O Most Great! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider!
