	I call upon Thee, O Lord, O Sovereign, O Exalter, O Judge! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider! I call upon Thee, O Lord, O Peerless, O Eternal, O Unique One! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider!

	I call upon Thee, O Lord, O Most Praised, O Holy, O Helper! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider!

	I call upon Thee, O Lord, O Beloved, O Glorious One, O Most Joyful! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider!

	I call upon Thee, O Lord, O Most Powerful, O Dweller, O Omnipotent! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider!

I call upon Thee, O Lord, O Ruler, O Self-Subsisting, O All-Knowing! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider!

	I call upon Thee, O Lord, O Spirit, O Light, O Most Manifest! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider! I call upon Thee, O Lord, O Thou Who Pervadest All, O Thou Who Art Known to All, O Thou Who Art Hidden from All! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider!

	I call upon Thee, O Lord, O Concealed, O Victorious, O Bounteous! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider! I call upon Thee, O Lord, O All-Powerful, O Helper, O Sustainer! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider!

	I call upon Thee, O Lord, O Maker, O Sufficer, O Awakener! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider! I call upon Thee, O Lord, O Raiser, O Gatherer, O Exalter! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider!

I call upon Thee, O Lord, O Perfecter, O Unrestrained, O Gracious! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider!

	I call upon Thee, O Lord, O Benevolent, O Withholder, O Creator! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider! I call upon Thee
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Sing, O My servant, the verses of God,
which you have received,
as they are intoned by those
who stand close to Him,
so that the sweetness of your melody
may enrapture your own soul,
and seize the heart of every man!

He who recites in the privacy of his room
the verses revealed by God,
the fragments of his words shall be scattered
by the omnipresent angels of the Almighty,
and they shall make the heart
of every righteous man throb.

Though he may at first suspect nothing of its effect,
the virtue of the grace in which he has shared
must, sooner or later,
change his soul.

Thus have the mysteries of God's Revelation
been ordained by the Will of Him,
Who is the source of power and wisdom.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Revealed from the Kingdom of Glory for the Days of Há.

In My Name, the Exile, the Wronged One.

O my God! These days have been ennobled by the name of ‘Há’ which Thou didst confer upon them. Methinks Thou hast made every one of those days a herald and a messenger, bearing unto men the glad tidings of the days in which Thou hast enjoined the Fast upon Thy servants, that every soul may prepare itself for meeting Thee, and may fix in its heart a place for Thee, and purify it by Thy Name for Thy descent upon it. O God of all existence and the Mighty One over all things seen and unseen! I entreat Thee by Thy Most Great Names, and Thy most exalted attributes, and the manifestations of Thy Cause in the Kingdom of Creation, and the dawning-places of Thy Revelation in the Realm of Everlastingness, to shower upon him who hath fasted in Thy love the rains of the clouds of Thy mercy and the effusions of the heaven of Thy providence. O Lord! Withhold not from them what is with Thee, as a reward for what they have done in Thy love and good-pleasure. I beg of Thee, O King of Names, and Maker of the heaven, and Creator of the heaven, and Sovereign of Names, and Upholder of the heaven, and Preserver thereof, to open the gates of Thy grace and mercy and providence unto the faces of those who have submitted to Thy command. O Lord! They are the poor, and with Thee are the oceans of wealth; they are the weak, and Thou art the Mighty by Thy power, and the Dominant by Thy sovereignty. O Lord! Thou seest their eyes gazing towards the horizon of Thy bounty, and their hearts turned towards the quarter of Thy gifts. Look, O my God, upon the hearts which Thou hast made as thrones for Thy sovereignty and as dawning-places for the effulgence of the lights of Thy love. Shower upon them, O my God, from the ocean of Thy generosity that which will make them the manifestations of this Name among Thy servants. O Lord! Strengthen them to that whereby Thy Cause may be exalted in Thy Kingdom, and then reveal from them that whereby their names may endure in Thy Kingdom and Thy Realm. O Lord! These are they who have been consumed by the fire of Thy love in Thy lands, and have witnessed every adversity in Thy path, and have tasted every bitterness, hoping for the honey of bounty from the heaven of Thy generosity. O Lord! These are they who have borne in His love what Thy servants and Thy creatures have not borne. O Lord! The devoted ones have been consumed.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
I implore Thee, by the splendor of the light of Thy glorious face, by the majesty of Thine ancient grandeur, and by the power of Thy transcendent sovereignty, to ordain for us, at this moment, the full measure of that which is good and befitting, and to destine for us every portion of the effusions of Thy grace. For in bestowing gifts, Thou sufferest no loss, nor doth Thy wealth diminish in conferring favors.
Glorified art Thou, O Lord! Verily, I am poor, whilst Thou, in truth, art rich; I am, indeed, humble, whilst Thou, in truth, art great; truly I am helpless, whilst Thou, in truth, art mighty; I, in reality, am abased, whilst Thou, in truth, art the Most Exalted; I, indeed, am afflicted, whilst Thou, in truth, art the Lord of grandeur.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text: Praise be to Thee, O God, my God! We have fasted by Thy command, and broken our fast through Thy love and good pleasure. Accept from us, O my God, what we have done in Thy path, solely for Thy Face, and looking to Thy command, without turning to any direction other than Thee. Then forgive us and our fathers and all who have believed in Thee and in Thy mighty verses in this Most Great and Most Wondrous Manifestation. Verily, Thou art the Omnipotent over what Thou willest, and Thou art the Exalted, the Mighty, the Chosen.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text: Praise be unto Him Who hath manifested, brought forth, sent down and made evident His Revelation, His sovereignty, His signs and His path [3.5s]... ...Peace and blessings be upon the day-spring of His names, the dawning-place of His attributes, the well-spring of His knowledge, the source of His cause, and the manifestation of His laws and commandments… Praised be Thou, O God, my God! These are the days whereon Thou hast enjoined Thy chosen ones, Thy loved ones and Thy servants to observe the Fast
prayers.notes: Base code entry for FK; sub-codes contain actual prayers

prayers.text: Glorified art Thou, O my God! I make mention of Thee in this moment wherein the doves of Thy sacred sovereignty warble their melodies and the rains descend [3.5s]

Words of Wisdom
# HE IS THE MOST EXALTED, THE MOST HIGH

The root of all good
Is reliance upon God and submission to His command and contentment with His good pleasure.

The root of wisdom
Is the fear of God, glorified be His mention, and dread of His might and scourges, and apprehension of the manifestations of His justice and decree.

The summit of religion
Is the acknowledgment of that which hath been revealed from God and the observance of that which hath been ordained in His immutable Book.

The root of might
Is contentment with that which hath been bestowed and sufficiency with that which hath been decreed for him.

The root of love
Is the turning of the servant towards the Beloved and turning away from all else, and that his desire be naught but what his Lord hath desired.

The root of remembrance
Is to stand firm in the remembrance of the Remembred One and to forget all else beside Him.

The summit of reliance
Is the servant's striving and earning in this world, and his seeking refuge in God, and confining his gaze to the bounty of his Lord, for unto Him return all the affairs of the servant in his return and abode.

The summit of detachment
Is turning towards the direction of God and approaching Him and gazing upon Him and bearing witness before Him.

The summit of innate nature
Is the acknowledgment of poverty and voluntary submission before God, the King, the Mighty, the Chosen.

The summit of benevolence
Is the servant's manifesting that which God hath bestowed upon him and his gratitude in all circumstances and at all times.

The summit of commerce
Is My love, through which everything is rendered independent of everything else, and without it everything is in need of everything else. And this is what hath been inscribed by the Pen of Resplendent Glory.

The summit of faith
Is paucity of speech and abundance of action. And whoso's words exceed his deeds, know ye that his non-existence is better than his existence and his annihilation better than his survival.

The root of well-being
Is silence and foresight, and withdrawal from mankind.

The summit of aspiration
Is for one to spend upon himself and upon his family and the poor among his brethren in his religion.

The summit of power and courage
Is the exaltation of the Word of God and steadfastness
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
All majesty and all glory, my knowing You,

My God, all majesty and glory, all sovereignty and splendor, and grandeur and brilliance are Yours alone. You give sovereignty to whomever You wish, and You take it away from whomever You will. There is no God but You, the Possessor of all, the Most High. You are the One who created the universe and all that dwells within it from nothingness. Nothing is worthy in Your sight but You, and everything else besides You is like refuse before Your holy throne, and nothing compared to Your glory. It is beyond my power to praise Your virtues. I have no other praise to offer than what You Yourself have said in Your Book (the Qur'an) praising Yourself: "No vision can grasp Him, but He grashes all vision. He is uncomprehending, yet All-Aware." My God, glory is Yours, and no matter how keen or sharp an eye or intellect, it cannot grasp even the smallest of Your signs. Truly, You are God, and there is no God but You. Only You can express Your attributes, and no praise but Yours reaches Your divine court, and no one but You can comprehend Your attributes. Glory be to You, and You are one whom none can describe but Yourself, for to adequately praise Your virtues or to comprehend the inner reality of Your essence transcends human capacity. If Your creatures were to describe You, or if anyone other than You claimed to know You, it would surely be far removed from Your glory. My God, my knowing You is solely because You revealed Yourself to me, and had You not revealed Yourself to me, I would not have known You. My worshipping You is because You called me to You, and had it not been for Your call, I would not have worshipped You.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is the All-Glorious!
O Mary! When Jesus, the Spirit, ascended from the placeless realm, the cage of being became empty of the praised bird, and the Nightingale of Eternity turned towards the wilderness of non-existence. The Divine Nightingale cried out upon the Tree of Lordship, tearing apart the pavilion of glory, and the Phoenix of Exaltation flew away from the bough of delight. The lofty heavens settled upon the dark earth, and lamentations arose from aching hearts. Sweet water turned to blood, and the expanse of the Supreme Paradise was mingled with blood. Verily, the luminous breasts of the friends are worthy of the arrow of divine decree, and the necks of ardent lovers are fit for the noose of infinite tribulation. Wherever there is a dart, it strikes the heart of the beloved; and wherever there is sorrow, it descends upon the souls of the companions. Lovers must have tearful eyes, and beloved ones may show grace and coquetry. If the Beloved utters a hundred laments, the Object of Love will only increase in cruelty. If you seek the draught of reunion, surrender your body to annihilation; and if you crave the wine of beauty, step into the valley of deprivation.

O Mary! Exchange grief for joy, and draw joy from the cup of sorrow. If you wish to tread the path of quest, be patient, do not scratch your face, do not shed tears from your eyes, and be not among the impatient. Wear the garment of submission, drink from the wine of contentment, and sell the whole world for a mere coin. Bind your heart to the divine decree and link it to the command of destiny. Open the eye of insight, and turn away from all save the Friend, for soon we shall encircle the sacred Presence, turn towards the Holy Court, hear the melody of Hijaz from the lute of Iraq, and join the Friend. We shall speak the unspoken, see the unseen, hear the unheard, and to the rhythm of light, we shall make the temple of the spirit dance. In the sanctuary of the soul, we shall arrange a delightful feast, and from the Cup-bearer of Majesty, we shall receive the goblet of Beauty. In remembrance of the Face of the All-Glorious, we shall drink the peerless wine. Cleanse the eye of water, sweep the heart of sorrow, free the soul from grief, and sing in a sweet melody:

# If swords rain down in the street of that Moon,
# We have bowed our necks; the decree is God's.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Praise be to Thee, O Lord. Forgive our sins, have mercy upon us, and enable us to return unto Thee. Suffer us not to depend on anyone save Thee, and grant unto us, through Thy bounty, that which Thou lovest and approvest, and that which befitteth Thee. Exalt the station of those who have truly believed, and forgive them with Thy loving forgiveness. Verily, Thou art the Helper in Peril, the Self-Subsisting.

"If someone commits an error against you, you must immediately forgive them."
"Strive... if someone falls into error a hundred thousand times, he can still turn his face to you hoping that you will forgive his sins; for he must not despair nor be sad and disheartened." - ’Abdu’l-Bahá
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text: In every circumstance, be patient and place your complete trust and reliance upon God.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O Lord! Thou seest the hands of hope stretched forth towards the heaven of Thy grace and Thy bounty. It is my hope that Thou wilt fill them with Thy favors and Thy gifts.

    O my God! Ordain for us and for our parents... "the blessing" Thou hast sent down. Then, out of the ocean of Thy wealth and Thy gifts, bestow upon us what Thou hast willed.

    Then, O our Beloved! Accept from us what we have done in Thy path. Verily, Thou art the All-Befriending, the All-Wise, the All-Knowing, the Ever-Forgiving, the Most Merciful.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O Lord, Thou art the eternal King, Creator of peoples, Builder of every bone and dissolver, Glory be to Thee! Count me among those who have turned away from all things other than Thyself, who have turned their faces towards Thee, and who, through the afflictions Thou hast ordained, have turned towards Thy gifts. I beseech Thee in Thy name, by which Thou didst call all the human race to the horizon of Thy majesty and glory, and Thy servants to the throne of Thy grace and Thy bounty.

My Lord, I have firmly grasped the hem of Thy loving-kindness, and have clung with constancy to the border of the robe of Thy bounty. Therefore, grant Thou that I may be enabled to turn to Him Whom those who have broken Thy covenant, and who believe not in Thee nor in Thy signs, have arrayed themselves in opposition—to Him Who is the object of adoration of all the human race—send down upon me from the clouds of Thy grace that which shall purge from my heart the remembrance of all save Thee.

My Lord, in Thy days, the fragrances of Thy raiment, and when the beauties of the light of Thy countenance have appeared, do not deny me the breathings of Thy revelation; for Thou art powerful to do that which is Thy pleasure; nothing can withstand Thy will, nor can anything frustrate what Thou hast decreed by Thy sovereignty.

There is no God other than Thee, the All-Powerful, the All-Wise.
He is the Mighty, the Forgiving, the Compassionate;

O God, my God! Thou seest Thy servants in the abyss of error and iniquity, O Thou, the Desire of the world, where is the light of Thy divine guidance? Thou knowest their helplessness and their weakness; O Thou, in Whose hand are all the powers of heaven and earth, where is Thy might?

My Lord, my God, I implore Thee by the beauty of the lights of Thy loving-kindness, and by the ocean of Thy knowledge and wisdom, that I may be among those who have honored the ordinances that are to be found in Thy Book, so that it be Thy will, and I beseech Thee by Thy word, through which Thou hast caused the peoples of Thy kingdom to believe. Ordain for me, O my Lord, what Thou hast ordained for Thy faithful ones, for those who, drinking the wine of the Divine Knowledge from the cup of Thy bounty, have hastened to fulfill Thy purpose, and to preserve Thy covenant and Thy new order; ordain for me also that which Thou hast ordained for them.

For Thou art powerful to do whatsoever Thou pleasest. There is no God other than Thee, the All-Knowing, the All-Wise.

O Lord, O Thou, the Lord of all mankind, ordain for me by Thy bounty that which shall enrich me in this world and in the world to come, and draw me nigh unto Thee. There is no God other than Thee, the Mighty, the Glorious.

O Lord, establish Thou our steps upon Thy path, and strengthen our hearts to obey Thee, turn our faces towards the beauty of Thy oneness, gladden our minds with the signs of Thy divine unity, that the existence of all living beings may sing praises unto Thee in the vision of Thy greatness, clothe Thou our bodies with the raiment of Thy grace, remove from our eyes the veil of sinfulness, and give us to drink the cup of Thy bounty. O Lord, let the holy, exceeding joy of prayer, a prayer that is exalted beyond all words and letters, higher than all speech and utterances, fill our souls, that all things may be transformed into naught before the manifestation of Thy beauty, through Thy merciful words and Thy divine life.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
In the Name of the All-Preserving, the Self-Subsisting.
# "O My handmaiden, speak forth with this utterance that hath shone forth from the Dawning-Place of the Mouth of the All-Merciful"

O my Lord and my peerless, unique God! I bear witness to Thy oneness and to the fact that Thou hast had no minister or assistant, nor wilt Thou ever have one. Thou hast ever been One and wilt ever remain so. O my God and the Beloved of my soul! Today is the day when the Euphrates of mercy floweth, and the sun of bounty shineth, and the heaven of grace is uplifted. I beseech Thee by Thy name, the Concealer, to conceal whatever hath been done contrary to Thy good-pleasure, and by Thy name, the Forgiver, to overlook our trespasses, and to adorn Thy servants and handmaidens with the robes of forgiveness.

O my Beloved and my Goal! Thou beholdest Thy handmaiden who hath turned unto Thee, and whose heart is enkindled with the fire of Thy love, and who hath clung to Thy self-sufficiency, and hath raised the hands of hope towards Thee. Grant her that which beseemeth Thee.

O my Lord! In this blessed day, deprive not Thy handmaidens, and record for all, through the Supreme Pen, the reward of reunion with Thee. Verily, Thou art the Potent, the Mighty, the All-Seeing, the All-Knowing.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text: Blessed is he who remembers his parents in conversation with God.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

th.cat: บทอธิษฐานเพื่อบิดามารดา

prayers.text:
Praise be to You, O Lord my God. Here is Your servant, who in Your hands has drunk of the waters of Your mercy, and tasted the sweetness of Your love on this day, which is Yours.

I implore You, by the glory of the manifestation of Your Names, to protect him from every tribulation that would hinder him from Your love, or turn his face away from beholding You; protect him, so that the snares of the heedless may be unable to turn him away from the path of Your good pleasure. I implore You to bestow upon him Your bounty, and to exalt him so that he may behold the world and realize that it is a fleeting thing, like the blink of an eye. In Your limitless power, protect him from all that You abhor.

Verily, You are his Lord, and the Lord of all creation.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
In His Name, the Most Glorious, the Most High, the Most Lofty!

Praised be Thou! O Thou, who art my God, my Master and my Lord, my Sustainer and my Hope, my Refuge and my Light. I implore Thee by Thy hidden, precious Name, known unto none but Thee: Protect the bearer of this Tablet from every calamity and plague, from every wicked person, whether man or woman, from the evil of the evildoers and the stratagems of the ungodly. Preserve him also, O my God, from all pain and suffering, O Thou in Whose hand is the dominion of all things. Verily, Thy power is all-embracing. Thou doest whatsoever Thou wilst, and ordainest whatsoever Thou pleasest.

O Thou King of Kings! O Thou Gracious Lord! O Thou Source of eternal bounty, grace, generosity and bestowal! O Thou Healer! O Thou Sufficient! O Thou Essence of light! O Thou Light above all light! O Thou Revealer of every manifestation! O Thou Compassionate! O Thou Merciful! Have mercy upon the bearer of this Tablet through Thy greatest mercy and Thy superabundant grace, O Thou Bountiful, O Thou Generous. Protect him also through Thy safeguarding from all that his heart and mind abhor. Thou art, verily, the Most Powerful of the Powerful.

May the glory of God rest upon thee, O thou dawning sun! Testify unto that which God hath testified of Himself: There is none other God beside Him, the Almighty, the Most Beloved.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
My God, my God! Your maidservant calls upon You, turns her face towards You, entrusts her hope to You, desires Your celestial bounties upon him, wishes You to reveal Your spiritual mysteries to him, and humbly prays You to cast Your light upon him from Your Divine Countenance.

My Lord! Open the eyes of my husband. Gladden his heart with the light of Your knowledge. Draw his thoughts to Your radiant beauty, reveal Your manifest glory before him, and restore his spirit.

My Lord! Remove the veil that covers the eyes of my husband. Send down upon him the abundant rain of Your bounty, intoxicate him with the wine of Your love, and make him one of those angelic beings whose souls soar in the celestial realm even while their feet walk upon this earth. Make him a lamp of precious jewel, shining with the light of Your wisdom among Your people.

You are the All-Precious, the Ever-Bestowing, the Generous.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
The Greenlandic text provided is the beginning of a Bahá'í prayer revealed by 'Abdu'l-Bahá.

O Lord! In this Most Great Dispensation Thou dost accept the intercession of children in behalf of their parents.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

th.cat: บทอธิษฐานพื่อตัดความผูกพัน

prayers.text:
The translation is as follows:

O my God, Thou knowest that tribulations are encompassing me from every direction. Thou art also well aware that none except Thee can remove or change them. I place my confidence in my love for Thee. Thou dost not bring afflictions upon any soul except when Thou wishest to exalt his station in Thy celestial paradise, or to sustain his heart with the impregnable stronghold of Thy power in this earthly life, that thereby the inclination to the vanities of this world may be cut off. I shall ever regard the remembrance of Thee as infinitely preferable to possessing all that is in heaven and on earth, and all that is therein. For Thou, indeed, knowest this.

　O my God, strengthen my heart in Thy obedience and in Thy love. Grant that I may be detached from all that pertaineth unto Thy enemies. Verily, I swear by Thy glory, I have no desire but Thee, and no hope except in Thee, and no fear save that which ariseth from the remembrance of Thee. My supplication unto Thee is that Thou mayest forgive me and all who love Thee, and mayest graciously vouchsafe unto us that which Thou hast ordained for them that are devoted unto Thee. For Thou art indeed All-knowing, the Gracious, the Bountiful.

　O Lord of Heaven and Earth! Thou art exalted far above the praise of all Thy creatures. Let peace be upon Thy faithful servants. And glory be unto God, the Lord of all worlds.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

ja.cat: ティーチング（一般の祈り）

prayers.text:
O Lord! Plant this tender seedling in the garden of Thy manifold graces, water it from the fountains of Thy bounty, and suffer it to grow into a comely plant through the effusions of Thy favor and Thy mercy.
Thou art the Great and the All-Powerful!
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O Generous, O Merciful

I bear witness to Thy unity and Thy singleness, and I ask of Thee that which shall endure with the everlasting dominion of the kingdom and the unseen. Thou art the Possessor of the kingdom and the Sovereign of the unseen and the manifest. O Lord, a destitute one hath turned unto the ocean of Thy riches, and a seeker hath approached the hem of Thy bounty; deprive him not. Thou art that Bountiful One, whereof the atoms of creation bear witness to Thy grace; Thou art that Bestower, whereof all that is possible confesseth Thy gifts. This servant hath turned away from all else, and clung to the cords of Thy generosity and the hem of Thy favours; in all circumstances, he looketh unto Thee and praiseth Thee, if Thou answerest, he is praised in his undertaking; if Thou rejectest, he is obedient to Thy decree. Thou art that Mighty One, before Whose manifestation all are seen to be helpless and incapable. O Generous One, leave not this servant unto himself. Thou art the Able, the Powerful, the Possessor of generosity and bestowal.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
# THE MOST GREAT, THE MOST GLORIOUS
This is a Book from Our presence unto her who hath believed in God, the All-Possessing, the Self-Subsisting, and desired her Lord when He came with manifest sovereignty. If thou wouldst gaze upon the greater prospect, thou wouldst behold the Lord of destiny in the hands of those who disbelieved in God, the Almighty, the Beloved. Despite all that hath befallen Him, His tongue speaketh of the mention of God, His foot walketh towards the desired aim, His heart stirreth at the remembrance of the Beloved, and with His finger the thread of existence moveth. Remember thy Lord, O My handmaiden, with this mentioned remembrance. O my God and my Beloved! I am a handmaiden from amongst Thy handmaidens; I have turned unto Thee and believed in Thee after the servants turned away from Thee. O my Lord! Inscribe me among the people of Thy pavilion of glory and the tents of Thy grandeur, then make me of those who were circling around the Throne of Thy grandeur and turned their hearts towards the precinct of Thy good pleasure. Verily, Thou art the Self-Sufficient in all conditions. Have mercy upon Thy male and female servants, then preserve them in the shelter of Thy preservation and protection. Verily, Thou art the All-Powerful, the Most Exalted, the Most High, the Great.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O God, and the God of all Names, and the Creator of the heavens! My prayer in Thy Name who is the Sun of Thy power and the Dawn of Thy manifested power, by Whom all made things are moved, all animated beings are resurrected, and all disembodied spirits are sustained – my prayer to Thee is to grant that I may cast off all attachment to any save Thee, that I may follow Thy purpose, that I may desire what Thou dost desire through the power of Thy sovereignty, and that I may do the work of Thy pleasure.

Here I am, in this moment, praying to Thee, my God, to bestow upon me the way whereby I may be led to relinquish all save Thee. Thou seest me, O my God, my face is turned towards Thee, my hands cling to the cord of Thy loving-kindness. Send down upon me Thy bounty, and write for me Thy inscriptions concerning Thy chosen ones.

Thou art able to do what Thou wishest. There is no God beside Thee, the Forgiver of sins in all ages, the Bountiful.-All.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
I beseech Thee by Thy Most Exalted Name to open the eyes of Thy servants, that they may behold Thee shining above the horizon of Thy majesty and glory, and that the croaking of the raven may not deter them from hearing the voice of the Dove of Thy transcendent oneness, nor corrupt waters prevent them from sipping the pure wine of Thy bounty and drinking from the everlasting streams of Thy gifts.

Gather them then around this divine Law, whose covenant Thou hast established with all Thy Prophets and Messengers, and whose statutes Thou hast inscribed in Thy records and writings. Raise them yet to such heights that they may hear Thy call.

Potent art Thou to do what pleaseth Thee. Verily, Thou art the Inaccessible, the Most Glorious.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Glory be to Thee, O God, for Thy manifestation of love to mankind! O Thou Who art our Life and Light, guide Thy servants in Thy way, and make us rich in Thee and free from all save Thee.

O God, teach us Thy Oneness and give us a realization of Thy Unity, that we may see no one save Thee. Thou art the Merciful and the Giver of bounty!

O God, create in the hearts of Thy beloved the fire of Thy love, that it may consume the thought of everything save Thee.

Reveal to us, O God, Thine exalted eternity—that Thou hast ever been and wilt ever be, and that there is no God save Thee. Verily, in Thee will we find comfort and strength.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
In the Name of God, the Most Holy, the Most Great, the Most Exalted, the Most Glorious!
#O God, the Desired, the Beloved, the Bountiful, the Compassionate!
Thou art the All-Knowing, from Whom no command hath ever been concealed, nor ever shall be, and Thou art the All-Informed, Who art aware of the secrets of hearts, souls, and breasts, and ever shalt be. Every pure deed returneth to Thee as light; Thy will maketh dust to transcend the heavens, and Thy majesty melteth the solid rock. O knowing God, Thy servant hath purposed a feast for Thee; accept it from him and graciously receive it. Thou art the Bountiful One from Whose heaven of grace none of the first or the last hath ever returned disappointed. Adorn this service of Thy servant Muḥammad Qabl-i-Ḥasan with the raiment of acceptance. Thou art the Overpowering over all things, and the Encompassing over earth and heaven. There is no God but Thee, the Gracious, the Generous.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

fa.cat: راز و نياز- شكر و ستايش

prayers.text:
O Thou, merciful Lord! Forgive the sins of my dear mother, absolve her imperfections, grant her a glimpse of Thy Providence and conduct her unto Thy Kingdom.
O God! She hath reared me from my infancy, nurtured me, but I have not been able to repay her for her services. Do Thou give her recompense. Destine for her eternal life and make her beloved in Thy Kingdom.
Verily, Thou art the Forgiving, the Kind, the Benevolent.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O Lord! Grant victory befitting Thy servants who endure in Thy time, for they have sacrificed their lives in Thy path. Grant that which will soothe their hearts, gladden their spirits, comfort them, and bring peace to their bodies. Aid their souls to ascend unto the presence of the most glorious Lord, and to reach the loftiest paradise and the luminous abode which Thou hast ordained for the wise and true of heart. Verily,

Thou knowest all things, and we are but Thy servants, Thy dependents, and the object of Thy compassion. O Lord, our Lord, there is none to have mercy on us save Thee, Whom we beseech. O Lord of this world and the world to come, none save Thee, do we implore for blessings and mercy. We,

are but a symbol of poverty, annihilation, helplessness, and utter destitution, whilst Thou art

the symbol of riches, independence, glory, sovereignty, and unending mercy.

O Lord, recompense us with that which is meet for Thee, in this world and the world to come, with manifold blessings which from Thy presence may descend upon us.

Verily, Thou art the Lord, the Lord of all things. We surrender ourselves unto the hands of Thy might,

and seek that which is with Thee.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

th.cat: บทอธิษฐานเพื่อชัยชนะของศาสนา

prayers.text:
O Generous, O Merciful!

    I bear witness to Your oneness, Your singularity, and I ask from You that which is eternal and everlasting in the kingdom and the dominion. You are the Lord of the Kingdom. You are the King of both the unseen and the seen.

    O Provider! A poor one has turned to Your sea of wealth, and a beggar has sought Your generous shade. Do not deprive him. You are so bountiful that all particles of the universe bear witness to Your mercy, You are so munificent that all beings acknowledge Your generosity.

    This servant, having abandoned all else, clings to the rope of Your mercy and the hem of Your robe of generosity, and supplicates You, and in every state turns to You, and thanks You. If You bless, You are the refuge; if You condemn, Your decree is bowed to. You are so mighty that before Your presence, others appear powerless and weak.

    O Generous! Do not abandon this servant to his own will. You are the Powerful and Capable, You are the Lord of Compassion and Generosity.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Glorified art Thou, O Possessor of existence and the Sovereign over the seen and the unseen! I entreat Thee by the Tongue from which the springs of wisdom and utterance have gushed forth in the contingent world, and by the Heart which Thou hast made the treasury of Thy knowledge and mysteries and the repository of Thy wisdom and revelations, to shower upon him who hath ascended unto Thee at every moment the drops of Thy mercy and the rains of Thy bounty. Verily, Thou art He Whose generosity every tongue hath testified and Whose grace every utterance hath acknowledged. Thou doest what Thou pleasest and ordainest what Thou desirest. Verily, Thou art the Almighty, the All-Praised.

Then we ask Thee, O God of the world, to ordain for those who are related unto Him that which will draw them nigh unto Thee in all conditions. Verily, Thou art the Self-Sufficient, the Most Exalted. There is none other God but Thee, the Almighty, the All-Bounteous.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
All praise be unto Thee, O my God! I thank Thee for having made me to know the Manifestation of Thy mercy, the Day-Spring of Thy grace, and the Treasury of Thy Revelation. I supplicate by Thy Name, through which the faces of those nigh unto Thee have been made white, and the hearts of those who are devoted to Thee have soared towards Thee. Help me, I implore Thee, to cling to the cord of Thy grace in all times and circumstances, to detach myself from all save Thee, to fix my gaze upon the horizon of Thy Revelation, and to carry out that which Thou hast ordained for me in Thy Tablets.

O my Lord, clothe my body and my spirit with the raiment of Thy bounty and Thy tender mercies. Protect me from whatsoever Thou abhorrest, and assist me and my household to be obedient unto Thee, and to flee from whatsoever stirreth up evil and passion within me.

Verily, Thou art the Lord of all mankind, the Possessor of this world and the world to come. There is no God but Thee, the All-Knowing, the All-Wise.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

th.cat: บทอธิษฐานพื่อตัดความผูกพัน

prayers.text:
(This prayer is to be recited only for Bahá’ís who are of age. “It is a compulsory Bahá’í prayer which is recited in congregation; it should be recited by one of the believers while all the attendees stand…” – Kitáb-I-Aqdas)  

O my God! This is Thy servant and the child of Thy servant who hath believed in Thee and in Thy signs, and hath turned his face toward Thee, and hath detached himself wholly from all save Thee alone.  

Thou art indeed the Compassionate, the All-Merciful. Forgive, O Thou Who art forgiving of the sins of men and concealest their misdeeds, as is meet from Thy bounty and Thy ocean of grace. Admit him into the sanctuary of Thy great mercy who was present before the foundations of earth and heaven were laid. There is no God save Thee, the Ever-Forgiving, the All-Bountiful.  

He who recites the prayer shall say:  

“Alláh-u-Abhá” (once)  

All of us, indeed, worship God (19)  

“Alláh-u-Abhá” (once)  

All of us, indeed, bow down before God (19)  

“Alláh-u-Abhá” (once)  

All of us, indeed, are devoted to God (19)  

“Alláh-u-Abhá” (once)  

All of us, indeed, praise God (19)  

“Alláh-u-Abhá” (once)  

All of us, indeed, thank God (19)  

“Alláh-u-Abhá” (once)  

All of us, indeed, have faith in God (19) 

 Bahá’u’lláh  

(If the deceased is a woman, he who recites the prayer shall say: This is Thy handmaid and the child of Thy handmaid………)  

 

O my God! O Thou forgiving of sins, Bestower of gifts, Remover of trials! Indeed, I beg of Thee to forgive the sins of those who have left the dust of their bodies and ascended to the spiritual world.  

O my Lord! Purify them from transgressions, remove from them the sorrow, and transform their darkness into light. Make them to enter the garden of joy, cleanse them with pure water, and suffer them to behold Thy light upon the most exalted summit.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

az.cat: Vəfat edənlər üçün namaz və dualar

th.cat: บทอธิษฐานให้ผู้ที่ถึงแก่กรรม

prayers.text: O God, my God! Protect Thy faithful servants from the evil desires and guard them with Thy loving eye against hatred, envy, and malice, preserve them in Thy unfailing Word, make them safe from the arrows of doubt, make them heralds of Thy glorious signs, illuminate their faces with the radiant beams proceeding from the source of Thy glorious unity, rejoice their hearts with the words proceeding out of Thy glorious kingdom, and strengthen their loins with the power proceeding out of Thy glorious dominion. Thou art the All-Bountiful, the Protector, the Almighty, the Most Merciful.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Say: O God, my God! Adorn my head with the crown of righteousness and clothe my temple with the raiment of righteousness.
Thou art indeed the Possessor of all gifts and favors.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Revealed in the Days of Al-Hā':
His utterance, Most High:
He is the Most Holy, the Most Great, the Most Beautiful.

Glory be to Him Who hath manifested Himself as He willed! Verily, He is the All-Powerful, the All-Sufficient, the Everlasting. These are the days of Al-Hā', and We have commanded all to spend thereon upon themselves and upon those who have turned towards this Exalted Station. Remember God, and then know their worth, for they speak of that Name by which God hath subdued the unseen and the visible. Verily, We have made them before the fast, a bounty from Us, and I am the All-Powerful to do what hath been and what shall be. Blessed is he who hath acted according to what he was commanded by God, and woe unto every heedless, rejected one. Verily, We have revealed the verses and sent them down unto thee on this sacred and praiseworthy Day, that thou mayest give thanks to God, thy Lord, and remember Him with a remembrance that may awaken the heedless.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text: O My Afnán! Call thou to mind those days when thou wert in the presence of the Countenance and didst hearken unto the Call of God, the King, the All-Knowing [3.5s]

O Lord my God, praise be to Thy Name! Through which time hath ceased, and resurrection become possible, and all in heaven and on earth trembled in awe; I pray Thee to cause to rain from the clouds of Thy favour and the tokens of Thy tender mercy, that gladden the hearts of them that have turned unto Thee and aided Thy Cause.
O my Lord, keep Thy servants and handmaids safe from vain imaginings and illusory thoughts, and grant them to drink of the cup of Thy knowledge that floweth from the hands of Thy bounty.
Thou art, verily, Almighty, Most High, All-Forgiving, Most Generous.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
* The person who wishes to perform this prayer should stand up and, facing God, look to their right and left as if expecting the mercy of the Merciful and Compassionate God. Then say:

O God of all names, and Creator of the heavens! I invoke Thee by Thy Unseen, Most High, Most Luminous Essence, and by those who are the Dawning-places thereof – turn this prayer of Mine into fire, that it may burn the veils that deprive me of beholding Thy Countenance, and into light, that it may show me the path to the sea of Thy union.

* Then raise his hands in supplication towards God, the Exalted and Blessed, and say:

Thou art the Desire of all worlds and the Beloved of all nations! Thou seest me turning my face towards Thee, and cutting myself off from all except Thee, and clinging to the cord of Thy sovereignty which hath set in motion all created things. I am Thy servant, O my Lord, and the son of Thy servant. Look Thou upon me, for I stand ready to do Thy will and to carry out Thy pleasure, and I desire naught else but Thy satisfaction. I swear by Thy Sea of Mercy and Thy Sun of Grace! Whatever Thou hast revealed, that is the object of my heart and the desire of my soul. O God, my God! look not at my hopes and my deeds, nay, look Thou at Thy will which hath encompassed the heavens and the earth. I swear by Thy Most Great Name, O Sovereign of all nations! I desire only that which Thou hast desired, and I love only that which Thou lovest.

* Then he should kneel and place his forehead on the ground (sajda) and say:

Thou art exalted beyond the description of all except Thee and beyond the comprehension of all save Thee.

* Then he should stand up and say:

Make my prayer, O my Lord, a fount of life, that I may live as long as Thy dominion endureth, and may remember Thee in all Thy realms.

* Let him again raise his hands in supplication (qunut) and say:

Thou art He whose separation maketh hearts and souls to melt, and from the fire of Whose love all the world is set ablaze! I swear by that Name through which Thou hast subjected all created things, grant me not to be deprived of those who are nigh unto Thee, O Judge of all mankind! Thou seest, O I
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

uk.cat: Молитви за навчання Віри зі Скрижалей Божественного Плану

prayers.text:
Faithfulness to Covenant

In the name of our Lord, the Most Holy, the Most Great, the Most Exalted, the Most Glorious!

O God, my God! Thou seest how Thy servant hath turned his face unto Thee. He longeth to do that which is commanded him in Thy Book. Ordain for him through Thy Most Exalted Pen that which shall draw him nigh unto the highest heaven. Verily, Thou art the Educator of the world and the Lord of nations. Thou art, verily, the Mighty, the All-Subduing, the Almighty.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
*The daily obligatory prayers revealed by Baháʼu'lláh are three in number. Every Baháʼí should choose one of these prayers and recite it daily as an obligation. The obligatory prayer should be recited according to the special instructions given with it.

*"In the context of obligatory prayers, 'morning', 'noon' and 'evening' mean the time from sunrise to noon, from noon to sunset and from sunset to two hours after sunset."

This prayer should be recited once every twenty-four hours, between noon and evening.

I bear witness, O my God, that Thou hast created me to know Thee and to worship Thee. I testify at this moment to my powerlessness and Thy power, to my poverty and Thy wealth. There is no other God but Thee, the Helper in peril, the Self-Subsisting.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text: God is sufficient unto me; He verily is the All-sufficing! In Him let the trusting trust.

Intercede, O noble and well-favored scion of a heavenly Father, for me no less than for the toiling masses of Thy ardent lovers, who have sworn undying allegiance to Thy memory, whose souls have been nourished by the energies of Thy love, whose conduct has been moulded by the inspiring example of Thy life, and whose imaginations are fired by the imperishable evidences of Thy lively faith, Thy unshakable constancy, Thy invincible heroism, Thy great renunciation.

O Mighty Lord! Thou seest what hath befallen Thy helpless lovers in this darkest of long nights; Thou knowest how, in all these years of separation from Thy Beauty, the confidants of Thy mysteries have ever been acquainted with burning grief. O Powerful Master! Suffer not Thy wayfarers to be abased and brought low; succor this handful of feeble creatures with the potency of Thy might. Exalt Thy loved ones before the assemblage of man, and grant them strength. Allow those broken-winged beings to raise their heads and glory in the fulfilment of their hopes, that we in these brief days of life may gaze with our physical eyes on the elevation and exaltation of Thy Faith, and soar up to Thee with gladdened souls and blissful hearts. Thou knowest that, since Thy ascension, we seek no name or fame, that in this swiftly passing world we wish henceforth no joy, no delight and no good fortune. Then keep Thy word, and exhilarate once more the lives of these, Thy sick at heart. Bring light to our expectant eyes, balm to our stricken breasts. Lead Thou the caravans of the city of Thy love swiftly to their intended goal. Draw those who sorrow after Thee into the high court of reunion with Thee. For in this world below we ask for nothing but the triumph of Thy Cause. And within the precincts of Thy boundless mercy we hope for nothing but Thy presence. Thou art the Witness, the Haven, the Refuge; Thou art He who rendereth victorious this band of the innocent.
O God! Thy nearness is my hope, and to commune with Thee, my joy; Thy love is my comfort and Thy name, my prayer.
